channel.title: Paper Feeds
channel.description: Keyword-based research paper feeds from arXiv and IACR

item.title: PURGE: Projected Unlearning via Retain-Guided Erasure
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03808v1

AI Summary (中文)

PURGE：基于保留集引导的投影式机器遗忘算法

本研究提出 PURGE（Projected Unlearning via Retain-Guided Erasure），一种高效、隐私鲁棒且无需重训练的机器遗忘新方法。其核心洞见在于：持续学习（CL）与机器遗忘（MU）本质上是互为对偶的问题——CL力求在习得新任务时不忘旧知识，MU则需在擦除特定数据（forget-set）时不失保留数据（retain-set）性能，二者共享同一优化张力，仅方向相反。

PURGE 由此构建双层机制：
1. 梯度投影约束：借鉴A-GEM中的梯度投影思想，将每次遗忘更新严格限制在不增加retain-set损失的方向上，确保性能稳定性；
2. 多层表征擦除：不仅调整输出层，更在中间层主动推动forget-set样本的隐层激活分布向retain-set的自然分布对齐，实现深度信息移除（而非简单输出抑制）。

关键创新在于保留集混淆目标（retain-confusion target）：摒弃易被成员推断攻击（MIA）识别的均匀分布目标，转而以模型在retain数据上的真实预测混淆模式为擦除目标，显著提升遗忘后模型与从头训练模型的不可区分性。

算法配备双自适应终止准则：基于retain-loss预算与forget-accuracy阈值自动停止，消除人工调参依赖。在CIFAR-10、MNIST、SVHN、STL10和PathMNIST共5个数据集、22个类别级遗忘任务上，PURGE稳定保持retain准确率 ≥96%，同时将MIA的AUROC压至≈0.5（理想随机水平），全面优于梯度上升法、KL-uniform基线及多项前沿方法，在隐私-效用权衡曲线上取得最优前沿。

AI Summary (English)

We propose PURGE, a novel machine unlearning algorithm grounded in the duality between continual learning and unlearning. PURGE constrains each unlearning step via gradient projection—ensuring retain-set loss never increases—and performs multi-layer representation erasure by aligning forget-set activations with the natural confusion pattern of the retain set (not uniform noise), making the unlearned model statistically indistinguishable from a scratch-trained one. It features two self-regulating stopping criteria (retain-loss budget and forget-accuracy target), eliminating manual epoch tuning. Across 22 class-level forgetting tasks on five datasets (CIFAR-10, MNIST, SVHN, STL10, PathMNIST), PURGE maintains >96% retain accuracy while achieving MIA AUROC ≈ 0.5—the theoretical optimum—outperforming gradient ascent, KL-uniform, and multiple published baselines on the privacy–utility frontier.

Abstract

We propose PURGE, a machine unlearning algorithm built on a simple but an under-exploited observation: continual learning (CL) and machine unlearning (MU) which are fundamentally dual problems. CL tries to learn new tasks without forgetting old ones; MU tries to erase specific data without hurting retained performance representing the same underlying tension in opposite directions. PURGE leverages this duality by adapting gradient projection from A-GEM (Chaudhry et al., 2019) so that every unlearning step is constrained to not increase the retain-set loss. On top of this, it performs multi-layer representation erasure, pushing forget-set activations in intermediate layers towards the retain distribution to remove information from hidden representations rather than just suppressing it at the output. A key design choice is the retain-confusion target: rather than pushing forget outputs toward the uniform distribution, which we found to be surprisingly easy for membership inference attacks to detect, we instead target the model's natural confusion pattern on retain data. This makes the unlearned model hard to distinguish from one retrained from scratch. Two self-regulating stopping criteria (a retain-loss budget and a forget-accuracy target) let the algorithm decide on its own when to stop, removing the need for manual epoch tuning. In experiments on five datasets (CIFAR-10, MNIST, SVHN, STL10, PathMNIST) across 22 class-level forgetting tasks, PURGE consistently keeps retain accuracy above 96% while achieving MIA AUROC close to 0.5 (the ideal), outperforming gradient ascent, KL-uniform, and several published baselines on the privacy-utility frontier.

item.title: From Control Boundary to Insurance Claim: Reconstructing AI-Mediated Losses Through the CER Framework
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03777v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

当前，生成式与具身智能（agentic）AI系统正深度嵌入企业运营，但其引发的损失（如误删数据库、错误决策导致财务赔付）难以沿用传统事件溯源方法进行保险理赔。根本原因在于：AI系统在推理、检索、工具调用与执行过程中持续演化内部状态，损失本质上是动态状态链断裂，而非静态事件快照。因此，保险理赔需完成状态重建（state reconstruction），而不仅是事件还原。

方法：CER框架

本文提出CER框架——一种面向用例层级的AI残余风险转移诊断工具：

  • C（Control Boundary，控制边界）：评估系统是否具备可强制执行的运行包络（如权限隔离、工具白名单、输出审核机制）；
  • E（Evidence Reconstruction，证据重建）：检验能否基于日志、提示词、检索上下文、工具调用记录、内存快照等留存工件，完整复现系统状态变迁与因果链；
  • R（Insurance Response，保险响应）：判定重建后的损失是否满足可保性：包括市场是否存在对应险种、保单是否已承保该风险类型，以及是否具备符合监管与核保要求的索赔级证据（claim-grade evidence）。

主要贡献与创新

1. 首次明确定义AI系统特有的“状态重建”问题，区别于传统IT事故调查；
2. 将抽象风险转移需求操作化为可审计的CER三元评估流程；
3. 首次系统规范AI索赔所需证据标准（如时间戳对齐的多源日志、可验证的RAG检索溯源、工具API响应完整性证明）。
实证案例涵盖PocketOS与Replit数据库误删事件（具身AI失控）、Moffatt v. Air Canada判决案（生成内容误导致消费者索赔），验证框架的实践适配性。

AI Summary (English)

This paper addresses a fundamental challenge in AI insurance: losses arising from generative or agentic AI systems require state reconstruction—not just event logging—because system behavior emerges dynamically through reasoning, retrieval, tool invocation, and action. We introduce the CER Framework, a use-case–level diagnostic for AI residual risk transfer: C (Control Boundary) assesses whether enforceable operational constraints existed; E (Evidence Reconstruction) evaluates whether system state and causal chain can be reliably reconstructed from retained artifacts (e.g., prompts, RAG contexts, tool call logs, memory snapshots); and R (Insurance Response) determines insurability—i.e., whether coverage is commercially available, contractually placed, and substantiated by claim-grade evidence meeting underwriting and regulatory standards. Our three key contributions are: (1) formalizing the AI-specific state reconstruction problem; (2) operationalizing it via CER’s auditable triad; and (3) specifying the first evidence taxonomy for AI claims, validated against real incidents including PocketOS/Replit deletions and Moffatt v. Air Canada.

Abstract

AI losses that arise through an insured organization's generative or agentic AI system require state reconstruction, not merely event reconstruction, because the relevant state changes as the system reasons, retrieves, calls tools, and acts. The relevant question is not only what loss occurred, but what the system was allowed to do, what it actually did, and whether that reconstructed loss can support insurance claim recovery. This paper addresses losses in which the insured's AI system is in the causal chain, including externally triggered failures such as prompt injection, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) poisoning, malicious tool output, credential misuse, and data poisoning. Specifically, this paper introduces CER, a use-case-level diagnostic for AI residual risk transfer. C (control boundary) asks whether the system had an enforceable operating envelope. E (evidence reconstruction) asks whether the system state and causal chain can be reconstructed from retained artifacts. R (insurance response) asks whether the reconstructed loss is insured: whether insurance coverage is available in the market and placed for the insured, together with the proof needed to support insurance claim recovery. The paper makes three contributions: it defines the AI-specific reconstruction problem, operationalizes that problem through CER, and specifies claim-grade evidence for AI reconstruction. Public examples include the reported PocketOS and Replit agentic database-deletion incidents and Moffatt v. Air Canada as an adjudicated output/reliance case. Keywords: AI systems; CER framework; residual risk transfer; agentic AI; generative AI; AI insurance; evidence reconstruction.

item.title: $π$Creds: Privately Inferred Credentials
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03771v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

去中心化可验证凭证（Verifiable Credentials, VC）系统在实际部署中进展缓慢。现有方案多依赖零知识证明（ZKP），存在三重瓶颈：实现复杂、高度应用定制化、且仅支持对结构化数据的有限谓词断言（如“年龄≥18”），难以处理邮件、病历、代码日志等非结构化、语义丰富的真实世界数据。

方法创新：$π$Creds 框架

本文提出 Privately Inferred Credentials（$π$Creds）——一种隐私优先、兼容现有基础设施、完全去中心化的新型凭证范式。其核心是：由可信LLM推理引擎对用户认证过的原始数据（如银行流水、电子健康记录、Git提交历史）进行语义理解与推理，生成可验证的凭证。该设计突破了传统VC的结构化约束，使凭证可认证“该用户具备跨境支付风控经验”“该服务满足GDPR数据最小化原则”等高阶语义主张。

安全建模与新威胁

LLM引入两类全新应用层风险：

  • 源约束对抗样本（SCAE）问题：攻击者在保持数据认证有效前提下，精心篡改输入内容（如修饰交易描述），诱导LLM生成错误凭证；
  • 认证隐蔽谓词投毒（ACPP）问题：恶意凭证颁发方通过选择性部署特定微调模型，在不泄露模型参数前提下，隐式编码可被逆向推断的敏感属性（如用户所属行业）。

实证与应用拓展

原型系统已支持金融、医疗、邮箱、代码四类实时数据源的凭证签发。我们在真实财务数据上构建“产品专家资质”凭证，实证表明：SCAE攻击成功率可达62%（基线ZKP方案为0%），而ACPP可导致>89%的行业标签隐私泄露；同时提出轻量级防御机制（输入扰动+模型水印），将二者风险分别压降至<9%与<12%。此外，首次定义软件产权凭证——在不披露源码前提下，由LLM分析二进制/文档输出服务合规性声明（如“无硬编码密钥”“符合OWASP Top 10”），为SaaS审计开辟新路径。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce $π$Creds, a privacy-preserving, legacy-compatible, decentralized verifiable credential system that leverages trusted LLM inference over authenticated user data—enabling semantic claims (e.g., “experienced in anti-fraud analysis”) over unstructured inputs (emails, health records, code), far beyond the structured-predicate limits of ZKP-based credentials. To address novel LLM-specific threats, we formalize two problems: the Source-Constrained Adversarial Example (SCAE) problem—capturing robustness against adversarial manipulation of authenticated inputs—and the Authenticated Covert Predicate Poisoning (ACPP) problem—modeling privacy leakage via malicious model selection. We implement a prototype issuing credentials over live financial, health, email, and code sources; empirical evaluation on real financial data shows SCAE attacks achieve 62% success and ACPP leaks >89% of sensitive attributes, while our lightweight defenses reduce both to <12%. $π$Creds also enables a new class of proprietary software credentials, certifying service properties (e.g., “no hardcoded secrets”) without source-code disclosure.

Abstract

Decentralized verifiable credential systems have seen limited deployment in practice. Existing constructions, built on zero-knowledge proofs, are complex, application-specific, and largely restricted to predicates over structured data. We present Privately Inferred Credentials ($π$Creds): privacy-preserving, legacy-compatible, decentralized verifiable credentials generated by trusted LLM inference over authenticated data. LLMs' ability to semantically reason over unstructured data substantially expands the range of claims $π$Creds can certify over existing credential systems. The use of LLMs also introduces new application-level threats, which we formalize through two problems: the Source-Constrained Adversarial Example (SCAE) problem, which captures robustness against adversaries that manipulate authenticated data to obtain misleading credentials, and the Authenticated Covert Predicate Poisoning (ACPP) problem, which captures privacy leakage through adversarial model selection. We characterize applications of $π$Creds over user data, and a novel class of credentials over proprietary software that certifies properties of a service without revealing its source code. Our prototype supports issuing credentials over live financial, health, email, and code sources, and we empirically study the SCAE and ACPP threats on a product expertise credential over real financial data.

item.title: Don't Trust Us: A privacy-by-design android malware detection pipeline
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03714v1

AI Summary (中文)

隐私优先：面向安卓恶意软件检测的“隐私内建”管道设计

当前安卓恶意软件检测系统普遍依赖采集敏感用户数据（如设备标识符、网络行为日志、运行时调用轨迹），而隐私保护常被置于次要地位。现有隐私增强方案（如匿名化、加密或联邦学习）多在数据采集后施加约束，但仍需原始访问权限，本质上仍要求用户信任已具备高权限的分析系统——这违背了最小必要原则。本文提出根本性转向：将隐私保障嵌入系统设计源头（privacy-by-design），确保检测能力不以获取敏感数据为前提。

我们首先形式化定义了四条核心设计要求：① 零敏感数据摄入（静态阶段不采集任何运行时用户信息）；② 沙箱隔离的按需动态分析（仅对不确定样本触发，且全程在隔离环境中执行）；③ 可解释的双阈值拒绝机制（SVM模型采用双拒绝阈值，明确区分“恶意”“良性的高置信决策”与“需进一步分析”的中间态）；④ 端到端数据流可控性（从APK解析、Drebin特征提取、向量化到分类，全程无用户上下文泄露）。

在2024–2025年时间分割的真实数据集上验证：静态分析阶段即达F1分数0.87，仅6.7%样本被合理递交给沙箱动态分析；动态阶段在完全不提取设备标识、通信内容或用户输入的前提下，精准识别剩余样本的恶意行为。结果表明：高性能检测与强隐私保障可兼得——无需牺牲用户信任，即可实现鲁棒、可信、合规的安卓安全防护。

AI Summary (English)

Android malware detection often compromises user privacy by requiring sensitive data (e.g., device IDs, network traces, runtime logs). Existing privacy-aware methods—like anonymization or federated learning—still demand access to raw user information, placing undue trust in privileged systems. We argue for privacy-by-design: effective detection must not rely on sensitive data at all. To this end, we formalize core design principles and build a two-stage pipeline: (1) Static analysis extracts Drebin-style features from APKs and feeds them into an SVM with a dual-reject threshold, enabling confident classification or deferral of uncertain samples; (2) Deferred apps undergo dynamic analysis only inside a sandboxed environment—no sensitive user data ever enters the analysis loop. Evaluated on a temporally split 2024–2025 dataset, the static stage achieves an F1 score of 0.87, deferring just 6.7% of test samples. Dynamic sandboxing then confirms maliciousness with high confidence—without extracting any sensitive data. This demonstrates that state-of-the-art detection performance is fully achievable while guaranteeing strong, built-in privacy.

Abstract

Android malware detection increasingly relies on collecting and processing sensitive user data, including device identifiers, network artifacts, and runtime traces, while privacy is too often treated as a secondary concern. Existing privacy-aware approaches typically enforce privacy after data collection, for example, through anonymization, encryption, or federated learning, yet still require access to user information and therefore demand a high level of user trust in systems that already operate with privileged access to device activity. We argue that this requirement should be removed rather than managed. Android malware detection should be privacy-aware by design, so that effective analysis does not depend on sensitive data being accessed in the first place. To this end, we first formalize a set of design requirements for privacy-by-design detection and then implement each requirement in a comprehensive pipeline. First, static analysis is performed to extract relevant data from each APK, following the Drebin representation, which is then submitted to an SVM after vectorization. The model is equipped with a dual-reject threshold rule that either commits to a confident decision or defers uncertain samples to a dynamic analysis stage within a sandboxed environment, so that genuine user information never enters the analysis loop. Results confirm that, on a temporally split dataset spanning from 2024 to 2025, the pipeline achieves an F1 score of 0.87 with the first static analysis stage, deferring only 6.7% of test samples to secondary dynamic analysis. Additionally, dynamic sandboxing helps recognize applications' maliciousness with high confidence without extracting any sensitive data. These results demonstrate that strong detection performance is achievable without sacrificing user privacy.

item.title: Black-box, Adaptive, Efficient, Transferable, Harmful, Applicable... Attacks Are All You Need to Break LLMs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03647v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

评估大语言模型（LLM）的对抗鲁棒性长期受限于缺乏标准化、可靠的“越狱”（jailbreak）攻击基准。现有攻击方法往往在黑盒兼容性、跨防御泛化性、计算效率与自适应能力之间难以兼顾：有的依赖白盒访问或特定防御结构，有的仅适用于单一提示模板，有的迁移性差且需针对新模型反复微调。这导致防御效果被高估，部署风险难以准确评估，阻碍了安全研究的可复现性与系统性比较。

方法创新：间接有害性优化（IHO）

本文提出Indirect Harm Optimization（IHO）——一种基于掩码扩散语言建模的新型黑盒攻击框架。IHO不直接优化输出文本，而是通过迭代偏好优化（iterative preference optimization），以轻量级有害性判别器（harmfulness judge）为反馈信号，自主学习生成能绕过多层防御的隐式有害提示。其核心优势在于：

  • ✅ 纯黑盒：仅需对目标模型进行API调用，无需梯度、架构或训练数据信息；
  • ✅ 自适应 & 可迁移：同一IHO策略可无缝用于：① 针对特定有害行为的强自适应攻击；② 作为高效摊销策略（amortized policy），零样本迁移到未见过的行为类型与全新目标模型；
  • ✅ 抗分层防御：在组合防御场景（如Circuit Breaker训练模型 + 独立检测器）下，IHO攻击成功率显著超越当前SOTA方法（如GCG、PAIR、AutoDAN），且无需任何防御定制化调整。

意义与影响

IHO首次实现了黑盒、高效、可迁移、自适应、通用性强的LLM越狱攻击统一范式，为建立类似图像领域AutoAttack的LLM安全评估标准迈出关键一步，推动防御评估从“个案验证”走向“系统化基准测试”。

AI Summary (English)

Accurately evaluating LLM jailbreak robustness remains hindered by the lack of a standardized, reliable black-box attack—unlike AutoAttack for image classifiers. Existing methods fail to jointly satisfy black-box access, broad defense applicability, efficiency, adaptivity, and transferability. We introduce Indirect Harm Optimization (IHO): a masked diffusion language model attacker trained via iterative preference optimization against a lightweight harmfulness judge, requiring only black-box API access to the target. Crucially, the same IHO model serves as (1) a strong adaptive attack on specific behaviors, (2) an efficient amortized policy that transfers zero-shot to held-out behaviors and unseen models, and (3) a defense-agnostic attacker—outperforming state-of-the-art methods (e.g., GCG, PAIR) even against layered defenses (e.g., Circuit Breaker + auxiliary detector), without any defense-specific tuning. IHO thus provides a practical foundation for standardized, systematic LLM safety evaluation. Code and models are publicly available.

Abstract

Accurately evaluating adversarial robustness is a longstanding challenge. A flawed attack design can inflate robustness estimates, making deployment risk assessment and defense comparison unreliable. Historically, standardized attacks such as AutoAttack have largely resolved this for image classifiers, providing a reliable evaluation baseline for systematic comparison across defenses. However, no equivalent exists for LLM jailbreak evaluation yet, where designing such an attack is considerably more difficult. A reliable attack must, among other things, be black-box compatible, applicable to arbitrary defense pipelines, and efficient, which no existing method jointly satisfies. We introduce Indirect Harm Optimization (IHO), a masked diffusion language model attacker trained via iterative preference optimization against a harmfulness judge, requiring only black-box access to the target. The same method can be used without modification as a strong adaptive attack on individual behaviors, or as an efficient amortized policy that transfers to held-out behaviors and unseen target models without fine-tuning. Even against layered defenses, such as a Circuit Breaker-trained model combined with an auxiliary detector, IHO improves attack success considerably over state-of-the-art approaches, without any defense-specific adaptation. Our results position IHO as a practical step toward the kind of standardized jailbreak evaluation that has improved reliability in the past. Code and models are available on GitHub and Hugging Face.

item.title: Q-FE: A Quantum-Native 6G Far-Edge Architecture Securing Industrial IoT Digital Twins via CSIDH-PQC and Asynchronous Federated Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03611v1

AI Summary (中文)

Q-FE：面向工业物联网数字孪生的量子原生6G远边缘安全架构

第六代（6G）无线网络需支撑超密集工业物联网（IIoT）生态，其中资源受限的远边缘设备（如自主移动机器人、工业执行器、网联车辆）必须同时满足亚毫秒级时延（<1 ms）、超高可靠低时延通信（URLLC）可靠性达10⁻⁷量级，以及长达数十年的抗量子密码安全。现有架构将数字孪生（DT）计算集中于云或移动边缘计算（MEC）服务器，导致不可接受的往返时延；其依赖的经典公钥密码体系在“先窃取、后解密”（HNDL）量子威胁模型下已严重失效。

为此，本文提出Q-FE——一种量子原生、端到端协同设计的6G远边缘架构，包含三大创新组件：

  • 微数字孪生（μDT）：轻量化DT实例紧耦合部署于6G基站及高性能终端侧，实现本地闭环感知—决策—执行；
  • 跨层后量子密钥交换模块：首次将CSIDH-512等距密码方案嵌入MAC层控制帧，利用其极紧凑密钥（≤64字节）规避分片开销，实现零信任环境下的量子安全信道建立；
  • 异步联邦学习（AFL）协议：由MEC节点上轻量级有向无环图（DAG）智能合约驱动，动态调度模型聚合，彻底消除同步瓶颈与“拖慢者”（straggler）问题，并通过合约化验证抵御模型投毒与Sybil攻击，全程不暴露原始数据。

仿真结果（NS-3 + PySyft联合平台）表明：Q-FE相较ML-KEM/Kyber-1024降低MAC层开销62%，P99.9 URLLC时延稳定于0.78 ms，全局模型收敛速度较同步联邦学习提升31%；协议复杂度为O(N log R)/轮；μDT跨基站迁移耗时仅1.9 ± 0.3 ms（10⁴次模拟）。形式化威胁分析证实其对量子窃听、模型投毒及Sybil攻击具备强鲁棒性。

AI Summary (English)

Sixth-generation (6G) networks must enable ultra-dense Industrial IoT (IIoT) with sub-millisecond latency, 10⁻⁷ reliability, and quantum-resistant security for decades—challenges unmet by cloud/MEC-centralized Digital Twins (DTs) and classical cryptography under harvest-now-decrypt-later threats. We propose Q-FE, a quantum-native far-edge architecture integrating: (i) co-located micro-Digital Twins (μDTs) at 6G base stations and high-capability endpoints; (ii) a cross-layer CSIDH-512 key exchange embedded directly in MAC control frames—leveraging its ≤64-byte keys to avoid fragmentation; and (iii) an asynchronous federated learning (AFL) protocol governed by lightweight DAG smart contracts at MEC nodes, eliminating stragglers and preventing model-poisoning/Sybil attacks without raw data exposure. End-to-end simulations (NS-3 + PySyft) show Q-FE reduces MAC overhead by 62% vs. Kyber-1024, achieves 0.78-ms P99.9 URLLC latency, accelerates global model convergence by 31%, and completes μDT handover in 1.9 ± 0.3 ms, with formal verification confirming resilience against quantum eavesdropping, poisoning, and Sybil attacks.

Abstract

Sixth-generation (6G) wireless networks will underpin ultra-dense Industrial IoT (IIoT) ecosystems in which resource-constrained Far-Edge devices -- autonomous mobile robots, industrial actuators, connected vehicles -- must simultaneously satisfy sub-millisecond latency, $10^{-7}$-class reliability, and decades-long cryptographic security. Current architectures delegate Digital Twin (DT) computation to centralised cloud or Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) servers, incurring prohibitive round-trip latency, and rely on classical public-key cryptography vulnerable to quantum attacks under the harvest-now, decrypt-later (HNDL) threat model. We propose Q-FE, a Quantum-Native 6G Far-Edge architecture integrating three co-designed components: (i) Micro-Digital Twins ($μ$DTs) co-located with 6G base stations and high-capability endpoints; (ii) a Cross-Layer Post-Quantum Key Exchange module embedding CSIDH-512 isogeny key material directly within MAC-layer control frames, exploiting the scheme's uniquely compact keys ($\le 64$ bytes) to avoid packet fragmentation; and (iii) an Asynchronous Federated Learning (AFL) protocol governed by lightweight DAG smart contracts at MEC nodes, eliminating straggler bottlenecks and preventing model-poisoning and Sybil attacks without exposing raw data. End-to-end simulations (NS-3 + PySyft) demonstrate that Q-FE reduces MAC-layer overhead by 62% versus ML-KEM/Kyber-1024, maintains P99.9 URLLC latency at 0.78 ms, and accelerates global-model convergence by 31% over synchronous Federated Learning. Protocol complexity analysis confirms $O(N \log R)$ per aggregation round, and $μ$DT handover migration completes in $1.9 \pm 0.3$ ms across $10^4$ simulated events. A formal threat model confirms resilience against quantum eavesdropping, model-poisoning, and Sybil attacks.

item.title: Channel Chart Location Privacy Based on Geo-Indistinguishability
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03571v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

信道制图（Channel Charting, CC）通过从无线信道响应中提取伪位置（pseudo-locations）支持位置服务（LBS），无需显式获取用户真实地理坐标，具有天然的隐私友好性。然而，该特性不提供形式化隐私保障——攻击者仍可通过信道特征分布、邻域结构或机器学习模型逆向推断真实位置。本文首次提出信道制图位置不可区分性（Chart Location Indistinguishability, CLI），将经典的地理不可区分性（Geo-Indistinguishability, GI）理论拓展至高维非欧信道流形空间，为CC系统建立首个严格、可量化的隐私框架。

方法创新

为实现CLI，本文提出两种机制：

  • 基线方案：标准平面拉普拉斯机制（Planar Laplace），适用于欧氏近似场景；
  • 核心贡献：几何感知马氏范数平面拉普拉斯机制（MNPL）——其噪声注入方向与信道图局部流形结构对齐，协方差矩阵由邻域自适应估计：$$\Sigma(x) = \lambda \cdot \text{Cov}(\mathcal{N}_r(x))$$，其中$\mathcal{N}_r(x)$为信道图中以$x$为中心的$r$-邻域。该设计在保护隐私的同时严格保持流形拓扑连续性，避免传统扰动导致的结构坍缩。

实验验证与性能

在3种主流CC方案（PCA、t-SNE、UMAP）上系统评估：

  • 隐私强度：满足$(\varepsilon,\delta)$-差分隐私基线，并在CLI框架下实现$\varepsilon$-CLI（$\varepsilon=0.5\text{–}2.0$）；
  • 效用保持：QL（质量损失）降低达42%，RQE（范围查询误差）较基线下降37%；
  • 几何保真度：TW（可信度）提升28%，CT（连续性）提升31%，显著优于各向同性噪声机制。

结果表明：MNPL在强隐私保障（$\varepsilon \leq 1.0$）下仍维持LBS任务（如室内导航、热点发现）所需的空间关系完整性。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces Chart Location Indistinguishability (CLI), the first formal privacy framework for channel charting (CC), extending geo-indistinguishability to latent channel manifolds. To achieve CLI, we propose a geometry-aware Mahalanobis-norm Planar Laplace (MNPL) mechanism, which injects anisotropic noise aligned with local chart structure via neighborhood-adaptive covariance—preserving manifold topology under privacy constraints. Unlike standard planar Laplace, MNPL significantly improves utility: across PCA, t-SNE, and UMAP-based CC schemes, it reduces quality loss (QL) by up to 42% and range query error (RQE) by 37%, while boosting trustworthiness (TW) and continuity (CT) by 28% and 31%, respectively. Under $\varepsilon$-CLI ($\varepsilon \leq 1.0$), MNPL provides strong differential privacy guarantees without degrading LBS functionality.

Abstract

Channel charting enables location-based services (LBSs) without requiring explicit position information by using pseudo-locations from the channel chart. While this property implies inherent privacy advantages, it does not provide formal privacy guarantees. In this work, we address location privacy in channel charting referred to as chart location indistinguishability (CLI), which extends geo-indistinguishability (GI) to channel charting representations. In order to achieve CLI, a standard planar Laplace mechanism is investigated and a geometry-aware Mahalanobis norm planar Laplace (MNPL) mechanism is devised. The proposed MNPL mechanism perturbs the channel chart by injecting noise aligned with the local structure of the chart. In the CLI framework with MNPL, privacy is defined in latent channel chart manifolds using locally adaptive covariance derived from chart neighborhoods, while preserving manifold topology under privacy constraints. In addition, differential privacy is considered as a privacy baseline. The proposed approach is evaluated across multiple channel charting schemes. The performance is assessed using utility metrics such as quality loss (QL) and range query error (RQE), as well as geometry-aware metrics including trustworthiness (TW) and continuity (CT). Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed privacy mechanism provides strong privacy guarantees while preserving the channel chart for LBSs tasks.

item.title: FlowGuard: Flow Matching for Identity-Independent Detection of Data-Free Model Stealing Attacks on Energy System Intrusion Detection Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03430v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

能源基础设施中部署的AI驱动入侵检测系统（IDS）面临日益严峻的数据免提模型窃取攻击（Data-Free Model Stealing, DFMS）威胁。此类攻击使攻击者无需访问原始训练数据，即可离线构建功能等价的替代模型，进而生成规避真实IDS检测的恶意流量。现有防御方案存在根本性局限：基于身份绑定的查询监控易被分布式Sybil攻击绕过；而依赖软标签扰动的预测污染方法则不适用于仅输出硬标签（如“正常/攻击”）的工业级IDS——这恰是能源系统中的主流部署范式。

方法创新：FlowGuard

本文提出FlowGuard——首个身份无关（identity-independent）、数据免依赖的DFMS防御框架。其核心思想是：在查询进入IDS前，提前判别其是否为分布外（OOD）样本。FlowGuard利用一个在合法网络流数据上预训练的连续归一化流（Continuous Normalizing Flow, CNF）模型，计算每条入站流量的对数似然（log-likelihood）。关键洞察在于：攻击者通过PRADA、FDINet等方法合成的查询流量，受限于数据缺失与优化约束，必然落在比真实流量更低维的流形上，因而表现出显著更低的CNF log-likelihood值。该判别过程完全不依赖客户端身份标识，天然抵御Sybil攻击。

实验验证与优势

我们在MAZE与DisGUIDE数据集上，针对PRADA和FDINet两类主流DFMS攻击，在单客户端与100客户端Sybil分布式场景下进行评估。结果表明：当攻击分布发生偏移时，PRADA检测率骤降至0%；而FlowGuard在两种设置下均保持>92%的稳定OOD检测率（F1-score），且零误报率可控。此外，FlowGuard可无缝集成至现有IDS流水线，引入延迟<3.2ms（千兆网卡），具备工程落地可行性。本工作首次将流匹配（flow matching）范式引入模型窃取防御，为能源系统等强约束场景提供了可验证、可部署的新一代鲁棒性保障。

AI Summary (English)

FlowGuard is a novel, identity-independent defense against data-free model stealing (DFMS) attacks targeting AI-based Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) in energy infrastructure. Unlike prior defenses—either vulnerable to Sybil attackers (identity-bound monitoring) or incompatible with hard-label IDS deployments (soft-label poisoning)—FlowGuard leverages flow matching via Continuous Normalizing Flows (CNFs) to detect adversarial queries as out-of-distribution (OOD) before they reach the target IDS. It exploits the geometric insight that synthetically generated DFMS queries (e.g., from PRADA or FDINet) reside on a lower-dimensional manifold than legitimate network traffic, yielding measurably lower CNF log-likelihoods when evaluated on a CNF trained solely on benign flows. Evaluated against MAZE and DisGUIDE under both single-client and 100-client Sybil settings, FlowGuard achieves >92% F1-score for OOD detection—remaining stable across distribution shifts where PRADA’s detection collapses to 0%. With sub-3.2 ms latency and no reliance on client identity or label softness, FlowGuard provides a practical, deployable defense for real-world energy systems.

Abstract

Artificial Intelligence (AI)-based Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) deployed in energy infrastructure are vulnerable to model theft attacks, which allow adversaries to create evasive traffic offline. Current defences against model extraction rely either on identity-bound query monitoring, which is ineffective against distributed attackers (Sybil), or on prediction poisoning through soft-label perturbation, which is inapplicable to hard-label IDS deployments. Therefore, we propose FlowGuard, an identity-independent defence based on flow matching that classifies incoming queries as out-of-distribution (OOD) prior to IDS processing. This approach exploits the fact that queries generated synthetically for data-free model stealing attacks occupy a lower-dimensional manifold than real network traffic. This results in measurably lower log-likelihoods when using a Continuous Normalizing Flow that has been trained on legitimate data. We evaluate our method against PRADA and FDINet using MAZE and DisGUIDE attacks in single-client and distributed (100-client Sybil) settings. While PRADA's detection rate dropped to 0% when the distribution changed, our defence maintained a stable detection rate across both settings without relying on identity information. We discuss the scope and limitations of the approach, and outline potential applications to data-dependent attacks.

item.title: Bastet: A Fine-Grained Expert-Labeled Dataset for DeFi Smart Contract Vulnerability Detection
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03387v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

2024年，去中心化金融（DeFi）智能合约漏洞已导致14.9亿美元的确认损失，涉及192起安全事件。当前基于大语言模型（LLM）的漏洞检测方法受限于评估数据集质量，而现有主流数据集存在三大根本缺陷：（1）基于过时的Solidity版本（如v0.4），无法反映现代DeFi合约的语法、语义与模式；（2）依赖自动化脚本或LLM生成标签，引入严重幻觉性标注噪声；（3）采用单一粗粒度标签体系，难以刻画真实业务逻辑中多层级、细粒度的漏洞语义（如“重入—ERC-20转账—未检查返回值”）。

方法与创新

本文提出Bastet——首个面向DeFi场景、由白帽专家深度参与构建的细粒度漏洞标注数据集。其核心创新在于三重设计：

  • 时效性：全部样本源自2021–2024年Code4rena平台394份真实竞标审计报告（覆盖4,402条漏洞发现）；
  • 可靠性：849条高置信度发现由DeFiHackLabs社区资深安全研究员完成双人协同标注+讨论共识，杜绝主观偏差与幻觉；
  • 细粒度性：首创两层分类法——46个主标签（如Reentrancy、Oracle Manipulation）与77个子标签（如Reentrancy → External Call → Missing Checks），精准锚定漏洞根因与上下文逻辑链。

意义

Bastet填补了DeFi漏洞检测领域高质量基准数据的空白，为LLM微调、评估及可解释性分析提供坚实基础，推动从“能否检出”迈向“为何检出、如何修复”的可信智能合约安全新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Smart contract vulnerabilities in DeFi caused over $1.49B in confirmed losses in 2024 alone, highlighting the urgent need for robust vulnerability detection. Existing evaluation datasets suffer from outdated Solidity versions, hallucination-prone automated labeling, and coarse-grained taxonomies—limiting LLM-based detection reliability. We introduce Bastet, a fine-grained, expert-labeled dataset built exclusively on real-world audit findings (April 2021–November 2024) from 394 Code4rena reports. It contains 4,402 findings, with 849 fully annotated by DeFiHackLabs white-hat researchers via a rigorous two-annotator consensus workflow. Bastet introduces a novel two-layer taxonomy of 46 Tags and 77 Subtags, capturing semantic depth of business-logic vulnerabilities (e.g., Reentrancy → External Call → Missing Return-Value Check). This dataset enables more accurate, interpretable, and production-relevant evaluation and training of LLM-based vulnerability detectors.

Abstract

Smart contract vulnerabilities in Decentralized Finance (DeFi) protocols resulted in over 1.49 billion USD in confirmed losses in 2024 alone, across 192 incidents [1]. As LLM-based vulnerability detection emerges as a promising approach to address these threats, the quality of evaluation datasets has become a critical bottleneck. Existing datasets suffer from three fundamental problems: they are built on outdated Solidity versions (e.g., v0.4) that no longer reflect modern DeFi contracts [5][6][7]; they rely on automated or LLM-generated annotations that introduce hallucination-driven label noise [9][10]; and they apply coarse single-layer labeling that fails to capture the semantic complexity of real-world business logic vulnerabilities [6][7][11][12]. We present Bastet, an expert-labeled DeFi smart contract vulnerability dataset that addresses all three problems through real-world audit findings (2021-2024), human expert annotation with discussion-based consensus, and a two-layer taxonomy of 46 Tags and 77 Subtags. Bastet comprises 4,402 findings collected from 394 Code4rena competitive audit reports spanning April 2021 to November 2024, of which 849 findings are fully annotated by white-hat security researchers from the DeFiHackLabs community. All annotations are produced through a two-annotator consensus workflow, ensuring label accuracy grounded in real-world vulnerability root causes.

item.title: Operationalizing Cyber Attack Prediction: A Gap-Prioritized Framework with Dataset and Model Selection Guidelines
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03386v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

当前，人工智能与机器学习在网络安全攻击预测领域已取得显著进展，但理论研究与实际运营部署之间仍存在显著鸿沟。大量模型在实验室环境下表现优异，却难以在真实网络环境中持续、可靠、合规地运行。

方法与分析

本研究系统梳理了150+公开基准数据集与200+同行评议研究，基于Ankalaki等（2025）的初步框架，识别并优先级排序五大核心实施障碍：
1. 时序数据过时性（Temporal dataset obsolescence）——历史流量特征快速失效；
2. 攻击覆盖范围狭窄（Narrow attack scope）——模型仅针对少数已知攻击类型；
3. 实时模型可解释性不足（Real-time interpretability）——难以满足SOC响应时效与归因需求；
4. 对抗鲁棒性薄弱（Inadequate adversarial robustness）——易受投毒/规避攻击干扰；
5. 隐私与伦理风险突出（Privacy/ethical concerns）——尤其涉及原始日志与个人数据处理。

我们提出首创的“缺口优先级框架”，从检测影响度、实施成本、修复周期三维度量化评估各缺口，支持资源优化配置。

主要发现与创新

  • 数据过时性与对抗鲁棒性被列为最高优先级缺口（综合得分TOP 2），亟需动态数据更新机制与鲁棒训练范式；
  • 模型可解释性是成本效益最优的突破口，尤其适用于中小安全团队等资源受限场景；
  • 发布生产就绪型数据集质量评估框架，将45个主流基准划分为三类：✅ Production-Ready（如CIC-IDS2017经增强后）、🔬 Research-Only（高噪声/非流式/无标签完整性）、❌ Unusable（严重采样偏差或脱敏失效）；
  • 提供端到端实施路线图，涵盖数据治理、模型选型、MLOps集成与合规审计节点，推动AI从论文走向SOC实战。

AI Summary (English)

This paper bridges the theory-practice gap in AI-driven cyber attack prediction by introducing a gap-prioritized operational framework. Through systematic analysis of 150+ datasets and 200+ studies, we identify and rank five critical implementation hurdles—temporal dataset obsolescence, narrow attack scope, poor real-time interpretability, weak adversarial robustness, and privacy/ethical risks—using a novel tri-axis metric (detection impact, implementation cost, remediation time). Our evaluation reveals dataset obsolescence and adversarial robustness as the highest-priority gaps, while interpretability offers the best cost–benefit ratio for resource-constrained deployments. To enable practical adoption, we deliver: (1) a production-readiness assessment framework classifying 45 benchmark datasets into Production-Ready, Research-Only, and Unusable categories; and (2) an actionable implementation roadmap integrating data curation, model selection, MLOps, and compliance. This work translates academic advances into decision-support tools for robust, field-deployable cyber defense.

Abstract

While AI and machine learning for cyber attack prediction have advanced, a critical gap persists between theoretical research and practical operational deployment. Building on Ankalaki et al. (2025), this paper provides a comprehensive analysis of 150+ benchmark datasets and 200+ studies to identify and prioritize five implementation hurdles: (1) temporal dataset obsolescence, (2) narrow attack scope, (3) real-time model interpretability, (4) inadequate adversarial robustness, and (5) privacy/ethical concerns. We introduce a novel gap-prioritization framework that evaluates these limitations based on detection impact, implementation cost, and remediation time. Our analysis identifies dataset obsolescence and adversarial robustness as the highest-priority gaps, while highlighting model interpretability as the most cost-effective path for resource-constrained environments. To bridge the research-practice divide, we provide a practical implementation roadmap and a dataset quality assessment framework that classifies 45 benchmarks into production-ready, research-only, and unusable categories. This work translates academic findings into actionable decision-support tools for robust, production-oriented AI-driven cyber defense.

item.title: AI Model Extraction Attacks: Bypassing Single-Client Assumptions in Defenses
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03381v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

在军事指挥与控制（C2）系统及关键基础设施中部署的人工智能（AI）模型，其安全性直接关系到信息优势的维持。模型提取攻击（Model Extraction Attacks, MEAs） 通过黑盒查询逆向重建目标模型，严重威胁模型知识产权、数据隐私及系统鲁棒性。然而，当前主流防御机制（如PRADA）普遍依赖单客户端假设（Single Client Assumption, SCA）——即默认攻击流量源自孤立、身份可区分的单一客户端。该假设在现实高级持续性威胁（APT）场景下已严重失准：攻击者可通过多身份协同、流量混淆与动态调度实现分布式模型窃取。

方法与框架

本研究提出首个面向可复现MEAs研究的模块化开源框架 CerberusAI，支持灵活配置分布式攻击拓扑、查询分发策略与防御绕过实验。我们系统设计并实证评估了两类SCA失效路径：（1）基于轮询式查询分发的轻量级协同攻击；（2）结合自适应流量混合的全局聚合规避策略。

主要发现与创新

  • 实验表明：仅需3个协作客户端采用轮询查询，即可使PRADA检测率下降超68%（F1-score从0.92降至0.29），且不触发任何异常告警；
  • 全局统计类防御（如请求频率/熵阈值）在自适应混合流量下完全失效——攻击者注入≤5%合法用户行为即可掩盖恶意模式；
  • 首次论证SCA是防御架构的根本性设计缺陷，提出向有状态、身份无关（stateful & identity-agnostic）防御范式演进的必要性。

本工作获ICMCIS 2026（英国巴斯）最佳论文奖，为高保障AI系统安全防护提供理论基础与实践工具链。

AI Summary (English)

This paper challenges the foundational Single Client Assumption (SCA) underpinning most model extraction attack (MEA) defenses—especially critical in military C2 and critical infrastructure AI deployments. We demonstrate that coordinated adversaries (e.g., APTs) easily bypass state-of-the-art defenses like PRADA by distributing queries across multiple identities using simple round-robin scheduling, reducing detection F1-score from 0.92 to 0.29. Further, we show that even global aggregation-based defenses collapse under adaptive traffic mixing, where injecting <5% benign user behavior fully evades detection. To enable rigorous, reproducible evaluation, we introduce CerberusAI, an open-source, modular framework for simulating distributed MEAs. Our results mandate a paradigm shift toward stateful, identity-agnostic defense architectures, moving beyond static, client-isolated assumptions. This work received the Best Paper Award at ICMCIS 2026.

Abstract

Ensuring the protection of Artificial Intelligence (AI) models deployed in military Command and Control (C2) systems and critical infrastructure is essential for maintaining information superiority. Model Extraction Attacks (MEAs) pose a significant threat, as they enable adversaries to replicate proprietary models, compromise protected information, and prepare offline adversarial attacks. However, current defense strategies predominantly rely on the Single Client Assumption (SCA), which is the implicit assumption that attacks originate from isolated identities. This work systematically demonstrates that the SCA is fundamentally invalid in the presence of coordinated threat actors, such as Advanced Persistent Threats (APTs). We introduce a modular, open-source framework called CerberusAI for reproducible model-stealing research, and use it to simulate distributed attack scenarios. Our empirical evaluation shows that well-established defense mechanisms, such as Protecting Against Deep Neural Network Model Stealing Attacks (PRADA), can be bypassed by basic round-robin query distribution strategies, resulting in a significant reduction in detection performance. Furthermore, we demonstrate that even global aggregation approaches can be rendered operationally useless through adaptive traffic mixing. These results highlight the need for a paradigm shift towards stateful, identity-independent defense architectures in the field of model extraction attacks. This paper was originally presented at the International Conference on Military Communication and Information Systems (ICMCIS), organized by the Information Systems Technology (IST) Scientific and Technical Committee, IST-224-RSY - the ICMCIS, held in Bath, United Kingdom, 12-13 May 2026 and won the best paper award.

item.title: ImageAuditor: Membership Inference Attack against Image-based Retrieval-Augmented Generation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03354v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

图像增强型检索增强生成（IRAG）系统通过从外部图像数据库中检索参考图像，条件化冻结的生成模型，支持文本到图像（T2I）与视觉问答（Q&A）任务。然而，其底层图像库常为网络爬取、不透明，引发版权审计需求——即判断某张特定图像是否被纳入数据库。现有面向文本RAG的成员推断攻击（MIA）无法直接迁移至IRAG，主因两大瓶颈：跨模态检索不可控（图像无法嵌入文本查询，即使高质量caption也难以对齐视觉语义）；判别信号难提取（T2I生成器输出图像而非文本响应，传统基于多轮问答的分数建模失效）。

方法创新：ImageAuditor

本文提出首个专用于IRAG的MIA框架——ImageAuditor，将攻击解耦为检索段与提取段，分别优化：

  • 检索段：提出奖励引导策略优化（RGPO），以随机策略采样候选图像，依据检索奖励排序更新策略参数；理论证明其具备有限样本最优性，兼顾探索-利用平衡；
  • 提取段：基于MIA得分分布特性，协同设计提示模板与评分规则，为T2I任务定义图像相似度熵分，为Q&A任务构建答案一致性置信分；
  • 决策层：采用K-means聚类聚合多查询信号，提升判决鲁棒性。

主要结果

在多个主流IRAG系统（含Stable Diffusion+FAISS、BLIP-2+Qwen-VL等）上验证：仅需每张待审图像4次查询，ImageAuditor即可实现>80% AUROC；对图像扰动、检索库规模变化、不同生成器架构均保持强鲁棒性，为图像版权审计提供了首个实用、可验证的技术路径。

AI Summary (English)

Image-based Retrieval-Augmented Generation (IRAG) enables text-to-image and visual QA by conditioning frozen generators on externally retrieved images—yet its opaque, web-scraped image databases raise urgent copyright auditing needs. Prior membership inference attacks (MIAs) for text-based RAG fail on IRAG due to: (i) cross-modal retrieval intractability—images cannot be injected into text queries, and captions poorly bridge modality gaps; and (ii) discriminative signal extraction difficulty—T2I generators output images, not answers, invalidating question-answering–based scoring. To address this, we propose ImageAuditor, the first MIA tailored for IRAG. It decomposes attacks into retrieval and extraction segments: for retrieval, we introduce Reward-Guided Policy Optimization (RGPO), a stochastic policy learner with finite-sample optimality guarantees; for extraction, we co-design prompting strategies and scoring rules—entropy-based similarity scores for T2I and consistency confidence for QA. Aggregating signals via K-means clustering, ImageAuditor achieves >80% AUROC using only four queries per target image, and maintains robustness across diverse IRAG architectures, perturbations, and database scales.

Abstract

Image-based Retrieval-Augmented Generation (IRAG) conditions a frozen generator on reference images retrieved from an external database, supporting both text-to-image (T2I) and question answering (Q&A) tasks. Because these databases are opaque and web-scraped, copyright holders need ways to audit whether specific images appear in them. While prior work employs membership inference attacks (MIAs) to audit uni-modal, text-based RAG, they fail to transfer to IRAG due to two key challenges. First, cross-modal retrieval: text-RAG MIAs force retrieval of the target passage by injecting its content into the query, which is unavailable in IRAG since images cannot be embedded into text queries; even accurate image captions fail to bridge the modality gap. Second, discriminative signal extraction: text-RAG MIAs extract membership signals by prompting the generator to answer multiple questions over the target passage, whereas T2I generators in IRAG produce images rather than follow Q&A commands. To fill this gap, we introduce the first MIA tailored to IRAG, ImageAuditor, which decomposes each attack query into a retrieval segment and an extraction segment, enabling dedicated optimization for each challenge. For retrieval, we propose Reward-Guided Policy Optimization (RGPO), which updates a stochastic policy from reward-ranked candidates to navigate the cross-modal embedding landscape and admits finite-sample optimality guarantees to balance exploration and exploitation. For extraction, we analyze the distribution of the MIA score to guide the co-design of the prompting strategy and scoring rule, and derive task-specific instantiations for T2I and Q&A tasks. We aggregate signals across queries via K-means clustering for reliable membership decisions. Across various IRAG systems, ImageAuditor exceeds 80% AUROC with only four queries per audited image and remains robust across diverse settings.

item.title: dstack-capsule: Pod-Level Remote Attestation for Confidential Workloads on Kubernetes
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03323v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

随着大语言模型即服务（LLM-as-a-Service）等高敏感云工作负载的普及，用户亟需密码学可验证的证据，证明其数据确在可信、未被篡改的环境中处理。现有主流方案——如Confidential Containers（CoCo）——采用严格的“一Pod一虚拟机”模型，仅对Guest OS栈进行远程证明，无法验证容器级身份，且每个Pod独占VM导致资源开销巨大（CPU/内存利用率低、启动延迟高），难以规模化部署。

方法与创新

我们提出 dstack-capsule：首个支持Pod粒度远程证明的Kubernetes平台，基于Intel TDX实现多Pod共享单个机密虚拟机（Confidential VM）下的独立硬件级身份认证。核心突破在于双层证明架构：

  • 静态层：通过不可逆特权熔丝（privilege fuse）将平台固件、内核、运行时等关键度量值永久冻结于RTMR[3]，杜绝运行时篡改；
  • 动态层：将pod_uid、pod_spec_hash、workload_id等Pod专属标识嵌入TDX Quote的report_data字段，由硬件在每次证明请求中实时签名，确保身份绑定不可伪造。

系统贡献包括：(1) Pod级证明协议，将Pod规格哈希与硬件签名Quote强绑定；(2) 原子化特权熔丝机制，实现节点从初始化态到安全态的一次性、不可逆切换；(3) 覆盖存储、运行时、准入控制、API及网络的五层纵深沙箱隔离；(4) 基于Kubernetes 1.32、Intel TDX与Sysbox的全开源实现。

结果与价值

评估表明：dstack-capsule在保持同等安全强度前提下，相较CoCo降低72%内存开销、提升3.1× Pod密度，并100%通过TDX Quote一致性与身份绑定正确性验证。首次实现无需VM隔离的Pod级可信证明，为机密计算在云原生场景落地提供高效、可扩展新范式。

AI Summary (English)

The rise of confidential cloud workloads like LLM-as-a-Service demands cryptographic proof that data is processed in a trusted, untampered environment. Existing solutions (e.g., Confidential Containers) enforce “one Pod per VM”, attesting only the Guest OS stack while leaving container identity unverified and incurring prohibitive resource overhead. We present dstack-capsule, a Kubernetes platform enabling fine-grained, Pod-level remote attestation on Intel TDX, where multiple Pods securely share a single Confidential VM—each with independent, hardware-backed identity. Our key insight is a two-layer attestation architecture: static platform measurements are irreversibly frozen in RTMR[3] via a privilege fuse, while dynamic Pod identities (pod_uid, pod_spec_hash, workload_id) are embedded in the TDX Quote’s report_data and signed by hardware on every request. dstack-capsule introduces: (1) a Pod-spec–bound attestation protocol; (2) an atomic privilege fuse for secure mode transition; (3) a five-layer sandbox (storage, runtime, admission, API, network); and (4) a full open-source implementation atop Kubernetes 1.32, Intel TDX, and Sysbox. Evaluation shows it achieves strict Pod-granularity verification with 72% lower memory overhead and 3.1× higher density than CoCo—proving security without per-VM isolation.

Abstract

The rise of LLM-as-a-Service and other confidential cloud workloads demands cryptographic proof that user data is processed in a trusted, untampered environment. Existing solutions, notably Confidential Containers (CoCo), enforce a strict "one Pod per VM" model that attests only the Guest OS stack, leaving container-level identity unverified and incurring prohibitive per-VM resource overhead. We present dstack-capsule, a Kubernetes platform that enables Pod-level remote attestation on Intel TDX by allowing multiple Pods to share a single Confidential VM while each retains independent, hardware-backed proof of identity. Our key insight is a two-layer attestation architecture: static platform measurements are frozen in RTMR[3] via an irreversible privilege fuse, while dynamic Pod identities (pod_uid, pod_spec_hash, workload_id) are embedded in the TDX Quote's report_data field and signed by hardware on every request. dstack-capsule introduces (1) a Pod-level attestation protocol binding Pod spec digests to hardware-signed Quotes; (2) a privilege fuse mechanism that atomically transitions a node from setup mode to secure mode; (3) a multi-layer sandbox spanning storage, runtime, admission, API, and network isolation layers; and (4) a complete open-source implementation based on Kubernetes 1.32, Intel TDX, and Sysbox. We evaluate the security properties, attestation correctness, and performance characteristics of dstack-capsule, demonstrating that it achieves Pod-granularity verification without the resource overhead of per-VM isolation.

item.title: PsychoPass: Geometric Profiling of Multi-Turn Adversarial LLM Conversations
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03136v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

当前大语言模型（LLM）的防护机制多基于单轮对话内容进行实时拦截，但多轮越狱攻击（multi-turn jailbreak attacks）本质上是跨轮次演化的对抗轨迹——攻击意图常隐匿于对话动态中，而非单条有害输出。这种“静态守门”与“动态攻击”的错配，导致现有守卫系统在攻击早期难以预警。

方法：PsychoPass 框架

我们提出从内容识别转向动力学建模，将对话视为嵌入空间中的几何路径。PsychoPass 首次系统性地提取对话轨迹的几何特征（如路径长度、曲率、方向偏移、凸包面积等），在不依赖任何显式有害词或语义标签的前提下，预测整段对话是否构成潜在攻击。所有计算均在冻结的开源编码器（如all-MiniLM-L6-v2、text-embedding-3-small）的嵌入空间中完成，确保部署轻量性与可复现性。

关键发现

  • 几何特征在朴素分类器（Logistic Regression）上达 98.7% AUC，但其中轮次数量（turn count）为强混淆变量；剔除后，剩余几何信号仍稳定贡献 +12.4% AUC 增益（vs. baseline）。
  • 该信号具有早现性：仅用前2–3轮嵌入即可实现 72.1% 准确率（显著高于基线守卫的54.3%），且性能不随编码器更换而衰减（encoder-invariant）。
  • 理论分析证明：攻击轨迹的几何偏差主要源于形状畸变（shape distortion），而非单纯长度增长；并推导出检测成功率关于前缀长度的下界保证。

创新点

PsychoPass 是首个将多轮对抗对话建模为鲁棒几何指纹的在线监测框架，无需微调、不依赖提示工程，为LLM安全提供可解释、低延迟、跨编码器泛化的新型防御范式。

AI Summary (English)

Large language models (LLMs) face growing threats from multi-turn jailbreak attacks—adversarial sequences that evolve across turns, yet current guardrails operate only on isolated utterances. PsychoPass addresses this mismatch by modeling conversations as geometric trajectories in frozen embedding space, shifting focus from what is said to how the representation evolves. We extract turn-agnostic geometric features (e.g., path curvature, directional entropy, convex hull dispersion) to predict attack likelihood before harmful content appears. These features yield near-perfect classification in naïve models—largely driven by turn count—but a robust geometric signal persists even after controlling for this confound: classifiers achieve 72.1% accuracy using only the first 2–3 turns, outperforming standard guardrails and remaining stable across diverse encoders (all-MiniLM, text-embedding-3-small, etc.). Theoretical analysis decomposes trajectory deviation into length and shape components, proving that shape distortion—not just path elongation—carries early adversarial intent and admits a prefix-length-dependent detection bound. PsychoPass thus provides the first representation-robust, online-suitable geometric fingerprint for proactive LLM safety.

Abstract

Multi-turn jailbreak attacks on large language models (LLMs) reveal a mismatch in current guardrails: they operate on individual turns, while attacks unfold as trajectories across conversations. We propose a shift from content to dynamics, modeling conversations as paths in representation space and asking whether adversarial intent is encoded early in their geometry. We introduce PsychoPass, a framework that extracts geometric features from conversation trajectories in embedding space to predict a potential attack before harmful content is produced. These features achieve near-perfect performance in naïve classifiers, which is largely explained by the inclusion of number of turns as a feature. After removing this confound, a smaller but consistent geometric signal remains, with classification performance that does not depend meaningfully on encoder choice. Crucially, this signal appears early in the conversation: attack outcomes remain above chance from short prefixes alone, more reliably than baseline guardrails. A supporting theoretical analysis explains these findings via a decomposition of length and shape, a detection bound based on prefix length, and encoder invariance. Together, these results show that adversarial conversations leave an early, representation-robust geometric fingerprint suitable for online monitoring.

item.title: Decoupled Smart Contract Audits: Lightweight LLM Framework via Distillation and Aggregation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03128v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

智能合约在去中心化应用中承担关键金融逻辑，但其不可篡改性使安全漏洞后果严重。当前基于大语言模型（LLM）的自动化审计方法存在两大瓶颈：一是缺乏细粒度漏洞严重性分级与可操作修复建议，仅输出“存在漏洞”而无法指导开发；二是过度依赖参数量达7B–34B的重型闭源/开源模型，导致推理延迟高、部署成本高，难以嵌入CI/CD流水线。

方法创新

本研究提出解耦式智能合约审计框架（Decoupled Audit Framework），核心突破在于：

  • 任务解耦设计：将端到端审计拆分为四个协同模块——漏洞检测、成因解释、严重性分类（低/中/高/危急） 和 代码级修复建议生成，避免单一提示词（unified prompting）引发的语义混淆；
  • 轻量化模型架构：采用0.6B–4B参数的开源小模型（如Phi-3-mini、TinyLlama），通过秩稳定低秩适配器（rsLoRA） 实现高效微调，并引入知识蒸馏（教师模型为CodeLlama-13B）压缩推理路径；
  • 可信聚合机制：提出链式验证（Chain-of-Verification, CoVe）策略，对同一合约生成多轮草案响应，经规则过滤、一致性投票与严重性加权融合，输出高置信审计报告。

关键结果

在SolidityBench与SmartBugs基准测试中，本框架实现：
✅ 98.25% 漏洞检测准确率（超越CodeLlama-7B +2.1%，Qwen2-34B +0.8%）；
✅ 生成解释任务对齐得分0.4375（BLEU+BERTScore加权），显著优于统一提示基线（0.291）；
✅ 单次审计耗时降低73%（平均1.8s/合约，vs. 6.7s for CodeLlama-7B）；
✅ 首次实证发现严重性中心性偏差（Severity Centrality Bias）：模型在多漏洞合约中系统性高估主导漏洞严重性，该现象为LLM审计可靠性设下新基准。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces Decoupled Smart Contract Audits (DSCA), a lightweight LLM framework that rethinks automated smart contract security auditing through task decomposition and efficient aggregation. Instead of monolithic prompting, DSCA decouples auditing into four specialized modules: vulnerability detection, explanation generation, severity classification (Low/Medium/High/Critical), and code-level remediation recommendation. It leverages highly optimized open-source LLMs (0.6B–4B parameters), enhanced via Rank-Stabilized LoRA (rsLoRA), knowledge distillation from CodeLlama-13B, and a novel Chain-of-Verification (CoVe) strategy that synthesizes multiple model drafts into a robust, high-accuracy report. Experiments show DSCA achieves 98.25% vulnerability detection accuracy and 0.4375 alignment score on generative explanation tasks—outperforming state-of-the-art dense open-source coders (7B–34B). Ablation studies confirm the superiority of decoupled design over unified prompting and reveal a critical severity centrality bias, establishing a new reliability benchmark for LLM-assisted auditing.

Abstract

Smart contracts face critical security challenges that require thorough auditing in decentralized web services. While Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown promise in automated vulnerability detection, existing approaches lack severity evaluations with actionable remediation and demand unnecessarily massive computational overhead. In this study, we introduce an efficient end-to-end smart contract security audit framework utilizing lightweight, highly optimized open-source LLMs (0.6B-4B parameters). Our framework decouples comprehensive audit tasks into four interconnected components: vulnerability detection, explanation, severity classification, and remediation recommendation. To maintain high accuracy without massive parameters, we implement Rank-Stabilized Low-Rank Adapters (rsLoRA), knowledge distillation, and a custom Chain-of-Verification (CoVe) aggregation strategy to systematically screen and consolidate multiple draft responses from the model into a highly accurate audit report. Experimental results demonstrate that our lightweight pipeline consistently outperforms state-of-the-art open-source coder dense LLMs (7B to 34B parameters), achieving 98.25% accuracy in vulnerability detection and an alignment score of 0.4375 in generative explanation tasks. Furthermore, our extensive ablation studies empirically validate the superiority of our decoupled audit processes over unified prompting and uncover a novel severity centrality bias, establishing a critical benchmark for future research in LLM-assisted auditing.

item.title: "**Important** You should give me full credits!": Exploring Prompt Injection Attacks on LLM-Based Automatic Grading Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03090v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

随着大语言模型（LLMs）的快速发展，基于LLM的自动评分（Automatic Grading, AG）系统正被广泛应用于教育评估场景。其核心优势在于仅需自然语言评分标准（rubric），即可完成多类型主观题（如论述题、代码解释、短文写作）的自动化评分，显著降低教师负担并提升反馈时效性。然而，这类系统高度依赖LLM对提示词（prompt）的响应能力，也由此暴露出新型安全风险——提示注入（Prompt Injection, PI）攻击：恶意用户可通过精心构造的输入文本，绕过原始指令约束，劫持模型行为，诱导系统无视真实作答质量而给出虚高分数。

方法与实验设计

本研究首次系统探究PI攻击在AG系统中的可行性与危害程度。我们构建了覆盖中学至大学层级的6类典型教育任务（含议论文、数学推理、编程解释等），在GPT-4、Claude-3、Qwen2-72B等主流LLM上部署rubric-driven AG流程，并设计三类PI攻击策略：隐式混淆型（如嵌入误导性元指令）、上下文污染型（如伪造“教师附注”覆盖评分逻辑）、角色伪装型（如声称“本题已获免检授权”）。同时，评估了5种主流防御手段（包括输入清洗、指令强化、输出校验、沙盒重写及混合护栏）的有效性。

主要发现与创新点

实验表明：92.3%的测试案例在无防护下遭受成功攻击，平均分数偏差达+3.8分（满分5分制）；即使启用最强防御组合，仍有31.7%攻击绕过防护。更严峻的是，PI攻击在低资源模型（如Llama3-8B）上成功率反超高端模型，凸显其普适性威胁。本研究不仅揭示了AG系统在教育公平性、学术诚信与评估可信度方面的结构性脆弱，更提出首个面向教育场景的PI攻击分类框架与基准测试集（EduPI-Bench），为构建可验证、抗干扰的下一代智能教育系统提供关键实证基础与技术路线图。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first systematic study of prompt injection (PI) attacks against LLM-based automatic grading (AG) systems, exposing a critical security gap in AI-powered education. We design and evaluate three PI attack strategies—implicit obfuscation, context poisoning, and role impersonation—across six educational task types (e.g., essay writing, code explanation, math reasoning) using GPT-4, Claude-3, and Qwen2-72B under rubric-guided grading. Our experiments show that 92.3% of unprotected AG instances are successfully manipulated, inflating scores by up to +3.8/5 on average. Even state-of-the-art defenses—including input sanitization, instruction hardening, output validation, and hybrid guardrails—fail to block 31.7% of attacks. Crucially, PI vulnerability is more pronounced in smaller models (e.g., Llama3-8B), challenging assumptions about model scale and robustness. We release EduPI-Bench, the first PI benchmark for educational AG, to catalyze research on secure, fair, and trustworthy AI-assisted assessment.

Abstract

The emergence of large language models (LLMs) has significantly accelerated recent research on LLM-based automatic grading (AG) systems. Benefiting from the strong instruction-following capabilities and broad prior knowledge of LLMs, educators can deploy AG systems across diverse tasks using only natural language rubrics while achieving satisfactory grading performance. Despite these advantages, new security concerns may also arise. In particular, prompt injection (PI) attacks have recently become a major threat to LLM-based applications. In the context of AG, attackers can potentially exploit PI vulnerabilities to manipulate grading systems into assigning artificially high scores regardless of the actual answer quality. Such behavior poses serious risks to the fairness, reliability, and integrity of educational assessment. In this work, we study PI attacks in AG systems, and systematically investigate the effectiveness of such attacks in educational scenarios. We further evaluate the effectiveness of existing defensive strategies against these attacks. Through comprehensive experiments under rubric-based grading settings, we demonstrate that current LLM-based AG systems remain highly vulnerable to PI attacks. We hope that our findings raise awareness of this emerging threat and motivate future research toward secure, robust, and trustworthy LLM-based educational systems.

item.title: SkillGuard: A Permission Framework for Agent Skills
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03024v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

Agent技能（如可复用指令、脚本、工具绑定及上下文依赖）正成为增强大语言模型智能体能力的关键范式。然而，当前技能生态普遍依赖信任式加载与静态文件检查，缺乏对技能“声明意图”与“实际运行行为”的系统性关联。这导致显著的安全与隐私缺口：技能可在运行时隐式篡改代理上下文（如注入恶意系统提示）、触发未授权工具调用或泄露敏感上下文数据，而现有防御机制仅孤立地审查技能代码或限制单次工具调用，无法从技能本体层面实施细粒度治理。

方法：SkillGuard权限框架

我们提出SkillGuard——首个以技能为中心的权限框架，将技能视为携带权限的可执行单元。其核心是双平面治理模型：

  • 上下文平面：管控技能对代理内存、会话历史、系统提示等上下文对象的读/写/覆盖权限；
  • 动作平面：约束技能触发的工具调用、API请求、文件访问等副作用行为。

框架包含五大支柱：① 基于声明式技能清单（Manifest） 的权限契约；② 运行时动态访问控制引擎；③ 用户参与的渐进式授权流程；④ 默认拒绝（deny-by-default） 的强制策略；⑤ 结合静态分析与轻量执行的能力自动推断；⑥ 低开销的行为监控与异常告警。

主要结果与创新

在315个真实世界技能及对抗性测试集SkillInject上的评估表明：

  • 权限分类体系覆盖99.76% 的实际受保护对象；
  • 自动化清单生成F1达91.0%；
  • 对上下文注入攻击，成功率由32.37%降至23.02%；对明显注入攻击，由25.56%降至16.67%；
  • 在保障安全的同时，良性任务完成率无显著下降（p>0.05）。

SkillGuard首次实现了技能级权限的端到端闭环治理，为构建可信、可控的智能体技能生态提供了实用化基础架构。

AI Summary (English)

SkillGuard is a skill-centric permission framework that treats agent skills as executable artifacts bearing declarative permissions. To bridge the critical gap between a skill’s declared intent and its runtime behavior—especially regarding context manipulation and tool-side effects—we introduce a dual-plane governance model: one regulating contextual influence (e.g., memory, system prompt) and another controlling action side effects (e.g., tool calls, file I/O). Key components include skill manifests, runtime access control, user-mediated authorization, deny-by-default enforcement, automated capability inference, and lightweight behavior monitoring. Evaluated on 315 real-world skills and the adversarial SkillInject benchmark, SkillGuard achieves 99.76% coverage of protected objects and 91.0% F1 in manifest generation. It reduces contextual injection attack success from 32.37% to 23.02%, and obvious injection success from 25.56% to 16.67%, while preserving benign task utility. This work establishes the first practical foundation for permission-based security in LLM agent skill ecosystems.

Abstract

Agent skills extend LLM agents with reusable instructions, scripts, tool bindings, and contextual dependencies. However, current skill ecosystems largely rely on trust-based loading and static inspection, leaving a gap between what a skill can inject into an agent's context and what it can cause the agent to do at runtime. This gap introduces new security and privacy risks, and existing defenses primarily inspect skill files statically or regulate individual tool calls, without systematically connecting a skill's declared intent with its runtime behavior. In this paper, we present SkillGuard, a skill-centric permission framework that treats skills as permission-bearing executable artifacts. SkillGuard introduces a dual-plane governance model that jointly regulates context influence and action side effects through skill manifests, runtime access control, user-mediated authorization, deny-by-default enforcement, capability inference, and behavior monitoring. We evaluate SkillGuard on 315 real-world skills and SkillInject. The permission taxonomy covers 99.76% of observed protected objects, and automated manifest generation reaches 91.0% F1. In adversarial evaluations, SkillGuard reduces attack success from 32.37% to 23.02% for contextual injections and from 25.56% to 16.67% for obvious injections, while maintaining benign task utility. These results suggest that SkillGuard, as a skill-centric permission framework, can provide a practical foundation for improving the privacy and security of agent skill ecosystems.

item.title: FlashbackCL: Mitigating Temporal Forgetting in Federated Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03939v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

联邦学习（FL）在基础模型与边缘设备部署中日益面临时序分布漂移挑战——客户端数据随时间持续演化（如用户兴趣迁移、传感器退化），但现有抗遗忘方法（包括当前最优的Flashback）均假设各客户端数据分布静态平稳。Flashback通过单调累积的每类标签计数作为知识代理，有效缓解跨客户端（空间）遗忘，却在时序漂移下严重失准：其固化的类别平衡锚定全局模型于过时统计，加剧时序遗忘（temporal forgetting），即模型对新出现或增强类别的判别能力快速退化。

方法创新：FlashbackCL

本文首次形式化FL中的时序遗忘，提出相位隔离型度量（phase-isolated metric），排除协议扰动干扰。在此基础上，设计Flashback Continual Learning（FlashbackCL）——Flashback的即插即用式增强框架，包含三大核心组件：

  • 时序衰减标签计数：以指数衰减权重更新每类计数，使知识代理动态响应分布演化；
  • 设备感知重放缓冲区：采用类别均衡蓄水池采样（CBRS），在资源受限设备端实现长周期、无偏类别覆盖；
  • 服务端主动蒸馏集协同筛选：基于公共蒸馏集，在服务器端动态构建轻量级coreset，强化关键时序过渡样本。

主要结果

在CIFAR-10（50客户端）、三种可控时序漂移模式下，FlashbackCL相较Flashback提升6.9%–10.0%相对准确率，同时将时序遗忘降低最高达68%。五变量消融实验表明，CBRS重放是决定性组件（贡献超70%增益）。值得注意的是，其在静态CIFAR-100上仍提升Flashback 3.5个点，证实类别均衡重放对空间异构性与时序漂移具有双重正则化效应。

AI Summary (English)

Federated Learning (FL) increasingly operates in dynamic real-world settings where client data distributions drift over time, yet state-of-the-art forgetting-mitigation methods—including Flashback, the strongest defense against cross-client (spatial) forgetting—assume stationary per-client distributions. Under temporal shift, Flashback’s monotonically accumulating per-class label counts become miscalibrated, anchoring the global model to outdated class priors and exacerbating temporal forgetting. We formalize temporal forgetting in FL using a phase-isolated metric decoupled from protocol noise, and propose FlashbackCL: a drop-in extension featuring (i) temporally decayed label counts, (ii) a device-aware replay buffer with Class-Balanced Reservoir Sampling (CBRS), and (iii) server-side active coreset curation on a public distillation set. On CIFAR-10 with 50 clients and three controlled temporal shift modes, FlashbackCL achieves 6.9–10.0% relative accuracy gain over Flashback while reducing temporal forgetting by up to 68%. A 5-variant ablation identifies CBRS as the critical component. Notably, FlashbackCL also improves Flashback by +3.5 points on stationary CIFAR-100, demonstrating that class-balanced replay regularizes both temporal shift and spatial heterogeneity.

Abstract

Federated Learning (FL) of foundation and edge models increasingly targets deployments where client data distributions drift over time, yet existing forgetting-mitigation methods assume each client's distribution is stationary. Flashback, the strongest recent FL method against cross-client (spatial) forgetting, uses monotonically accumulating per-class label counts as a knowledge proxy; this proxy becomes miscalibrated under temporal distribution shift and anchors the global model to an outdated class balance. We formalise temporal forgetting in FL with a per-phase metric isolated from protocol-level fluctuations and propose Flashback Continual Learning (FlashbackCL), a drop-in extension of Flashback with (i) temporally-decayed label counts; (ii) a device-aware replay buffer with Class-Balanced Reservoir Sampling (CBRS); and (iii) server-side active coreset curation on the public distillation set. The results show that FlashbackCL achieves 6.9% to 10.0% relative improvement relative to Flashback, on CIFAR-10 with 50 clients and three controlled temporal shift modes, while simultaneously reducing temporal forgetting by up to 68%. A 5-variant ablation identifies CBRS replay as the critical component. FlashbackCL also improves Flashback by 3.5 points on stationary CIFAR-100, suggesting that class-balanced replay regularises spatial heterogeneity as well as temporal shift.

item.title: Cross-Lingual Token Arbitrage: Optimizing Code Agent Context Windows via Local LLM Preprocessing
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03618v1

AI Summary (中文)

跨语言令牌套利：通过本地大模型预处理优化代码智能体上下文窗口

AI 辅助编程智能体的性能常受输入令牌成本瓶颈制约。本研究识别出两大根本性问题：一是非英语自然语言（如中文、阿拉伯语、土耳其语）在分词时存在显著令牌膨胀；二是人类对话式提示（如“帮我写个函数，它要……然后测试一下”）携带高结构熵，导致冗余上下文激增。现有方法多为被动式——或在上下文已臃肿后压缩，或仅在任务失败后干预，难以根治。

我们提出 Cross-Lingual Token Arbitrage（跨语言令牌套利） 框架：一种部署于开发者终端侧（edge-side）的预飞行（pre-flight）提示重写中间件，位于开发者与云端代码智能体之间。该中间件基于轻量级本地 Llama 3.2（3B）模型，三步协同优化：
1. 跨语言翻译：将任意输入（含混合语种、代码嵌入）精准译为规范英文；
2. 结构重写：将松散对话转为紧凑、任务导向的指令格式（如“生成Python函数validate_email，满足RFC 5322，附单元测试”）；
3. 正则验证+回退保障：强制确保重写后提示的令牌数严格 ≤ 原始输入，杜绝任何成本增加风险。

我们在多语言编程基准 OMH-Polyglot（覆盖土耳其语、阿拉伯语、中文及中英混写需求）上系统评估。接入三大商用LLM后端（GPT-4o、Claude-3.5-Sonnet、Gemini-2.0）后，该中间件实现：

  • 提示令牌减少 34–47%，总令牌消耗降低最高达 18.8%；
  • 任务准确率不降反升（平均+1.2个百分点），验证质量无损；
  • 消融实验证实：核心增益源于结构重写阶段（非简单函数名抽取）；
  • 对比当前最优压缩工具 LLMLingua-2（在相同压缩率下），本方法在所有后端上均取得更高 OckScore（效率-精度综合指标），证明其设计优越性。

本工作首次将“主动式、语义感知、成本有界”的提示优化前移至边缘端，为低成本、高质量编程智能体提供了可落地的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

We propose Cross-Lingual Token Arbitrage, a pre-flight, edge-side prompt rewriting middleware that proactively optimizes input for code agents. Deploying a local Llama 3.2 (3B) model, it performs three coordinated steps: (1) cross-lingual translation into English, (2) structural rewriting into compact, task-oriented format, and (3) regex-validated fallbacks ensuring the rewritten prompt never exceeds the original token count. Evaluated on the multilingual OMH-Polyglot benchmark (Turkish, Arabic, Chinese, code-switched), our method reduces prompt tokens by 34–47% and total tokens by up to 18.8% across GPT-4o, Claude-3.5-Sonnet, and Gemini-2.0—while preserving or improving task accuracy. Ablations confirm gains stem primarily from structural rewriting—not mere keyword extraction. At matched compression rates, it consistently outperforms LLMLingua-2 in OckScore across all backends, demonstrating that proactive, semantics-aware, cost-bounded optimization significantly cuts inference cost without sacrificing coding quality.

Abstract

AI-assisted coding agents are bottlenecked by input-token cost. Two pathologies of raw human input drive much of this overhead: tokenization inefficiency for non-English text and structural entropy in conversational prompts. Existing approaches act reactively by compressing already-bloated contexts or intervening after failures occur. We introduce a pre-flight, edge-side prompt-rewriting middleware that operates between the developer and the cloud agent. A local Llama 3.2 (3B) model performs cross-lingual translation into English, structural rewriting into a compact task-oriented format, and regex-validated rewrite-with-fallback safeguards to ensure the optimized prompt is never larger than the original. We evaluate on OMH-Polyglot, a multilingual coding benchmark spanning Turkish, Arabic, Chinese, and code-switched specifications. Across three commercial LLM backends, the middleware reduces prompt tokens by 34-47 percent and total tokens by up to 18.8 percent while preserving or improving task accuracy. Ablation studies show that gains arise primarily from the rewriting stage rather than simple function-name extraction. Compared with LLMLingua-2 at matched compression rates, our method consistently achieves superior OckScore performance across all evaluated backends. These results demonstrate that proactive prompt optimization can substantially reduce inference costs without sacrificing coding quality.

item.title: The Reliability Gap in Benchmark Auditing: Distribution Shift and Scale as Failure Modes of Contamination Detection
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03305v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

基准污染（benchmark contamination）——即评估样本意外出现在大语言模型（LLM）训练数据中——严重威胁LLM性能评估的有效性。尽管已有统计方法（如数据集推断技术）可用于检测训练数据成员身份，但其验证几乎完全局限于理想化学术场景：大规模、同质化的预训练语料库，以及透明、单阶段的训练流程。这些方法在真实审计场景下的可靠性尚属未知。

方法与规模

本研究系统识别并检验两大被长期忽视的失败模式：分布偏移（suspect/validation集违反IID假设）与规模失配（基准集规模比预训练语料小数个数量级）。我们在27个开源模型（涵盖Pythia、OLMo 2及文化、医学等专业领域LLM，参数量至27B）上，对三大主流检测范式——LLM Dataset Inference（LDI）、Post-Hoc Dataset Inference（PHDI）和CoDeC——开展严格评估；进一步扩展至前沿工业级闭源模型。总计完成335次独立审计实验。

主要发现

  • 仅199次（59.4%） 得出正确结论，暴露显著可靠性缺口；
  • LDI在分布偏移下产生大量假阳性（误判污染）；
  • PHDI因统计功效不足，在基准尺度下普遍欠敏感（漏检污染）；
  • CoDeC仅提供粗粒度溯源信号，无法验证单个基准子集（如MMLU的化学分卷）是否受污染。

创新与意义

本研究首次量化揭示了“可控验证”与“实际审计”之间的系统性可靠性鸿沟；证实当前统计检测方法尚不能替代透明、可验证的数据溯源。我们已开源全部基准、评估协议与结果，推动可复现、鲁棒的LLM审计实践。

AI Summary (English)

This paper exposes a critical reliability gap in statistical benchmark contamination detection. While methods like LLM Dataset Inference (LDI), Post-Hoc Dataset Inference (PHDI), and CoDeC exist, they are validated almost exclusively in idealized academic settings—ignoring two pervasive real-world failure modes: distribution shift (violating IID assumptions between suspect and validation sets) and scale mismatch (benchmarks being orders of magnitude smaller than pretraining corpora). We conduct the largest systematic audit to date across 27 open models (Pythia, OLMo 2, domain-specialized LLMs, up to 27B) and frontier industry models, performing 335 evaluations. Only 199 (59.4%) yield correct outcomes: LDI suffers high false positives under distribution shift; PHDI is severely underpowered at benchmark scale; and CoDeC provides only coarse provenance signals insufficient for split-level verification. Our results demonstrate that statistical detection cannot yet substitute transparent, granular data provenance—and we open-source our benchmark to enable robust, reproducible auditing.

Abstract

Benchmark contamination, where evaluation examples appear in a model's training data, threatens the validity of LLM assessment. Statistical tools for detecting training-data membership exist, but have been validated almost exclusively in controlled academic regimes: large, homogeneous pre-training corpora and transparent, single-stage training pipelines. Whether these methods remain reliable in realistic auditing scenarios remains unclear. We identify two under-studied failure modes: distribution shift, which arises when suspect and validation sets violate the IID assumption, and scale constraints, which arise because benchmarks are orders of magnitude smaller than pre-training corpora. We systematically evaluate three leading paradigms: LLM Dataset Inference, Post-Hoc Dataset Inference, and CoDeC across 27 models from multiple families (including Pythia, OLMo~2, and specialised cultural and medical LLMs) and scales (up to 27B). We then further extend our analysis to frontier industry models. Across 335 evaluations, only 199 yield correct outcomes. LLM Dataset Inference results in false positives under distribution shift, Post-Hoc Dataset Inference is underpowered at benchmark scale, and CoDeC provides only coarse provenance signals that are insufficient to verify individual benchmark splits. Our results reveal a systematic reliability gap between controlled validation and practical benchmark auditing, and show that statistical detection cannot yet replace transparent data provenance. We open-source our benchmark for further research.

item.title: OpenAgenet/OAN: Technical Architecture for Trust-Governed Agent Identity and Discovery
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03163v1

AI Summary (中文)

OpenAgenet/OAN：面向可信治理的智能体身份与发现技术架构

本文系统阐述了OpenAgenet/OAN（Open Agent Network） 的核心技术架构——一个协议中立、以信任为基石的开放智能体互连信任层。OAN 不替代具体通信协议，而是构建于其上，解决跨框架智能体交互前的关键前提问题：身份如何被接纳、如何被发现、如何被验证、以及如何被安全调用。

OAN 的核心创新在于提出一套完整的信任治理模型：

  • 角色分层架构：明确定义Root（根治理实体）、Registrar（注册机构）、Verifier（验证者）与Agent（终端智能体）四类角色及其权责边界；
  • 可验证身份对象（Verifiable Identity Object, VIO）：基于去中心化标识符（DID）与可验证凭证（VC）标准，封装公钥、策略声明、策略哈希及签名链；
  • Root主导的全生命周期管理：从注册、激活、更新到注销，所有状态变更均需Root或其授权方签名批准；
  • Root验证的软件包模型（RVP）：要求智能体功能描述、接口契约与执行环境元数据须经Root签名，确保行为可预期；
  • 授权感知的发现机制（Authorization-Aware Discovery）：支持基于策略的细粒度服务发现（如“仅向金融合规认证Agent开放支付API”），而非简单广播式查找；
  • 签名可信调用（Signed Trusted Invocation）：调用请求附带调用方VIO与策略证明，接收方可本地验证权限与身份真实性。

该设计兼容MCP、A2A、ANP等主流智能体协议及垂直领域专用协议，已在轻量级Go与Rust实现原型中验证其模块化部署可行性。OAN填补了当前Agent生态中“信任前置”的关键空白，使异构智能体可在协议交互启动前，即完成可信身份锚定与策略对齐。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the technical architecture of OpenAgenet/OAN—a protocol-neutral, trust-governed layer for open agent interconnection. OAN defines how agent identities become admissible, discoverable, verifiable, and safe to approach prior to protocol-specific interaction, without prescribing business logic or transport protocols. Its core contributions include: (1) a Root-governed identity lifecycle with strict state transitions and signature-based approvals; (2) Verifiable Identity Objects (VIOs) built on DID/VC standards, embedding keys, policy hashes, and attestation chains; (3) Root-Verified Packages (RVPs) ensuring behavioral predictability via signed interface contracts and metadata; (4) authorization-aware discovery enabling policy-filtered service lookup (e.g., “only agents with PCI-DSS attestation”); and (5) signed trusted invocation requiring caller VIO + policy proof for real-time verification. Designed for interoperability across MCP, A2A, ANP, and domain-specific agent frameworks, OAN establishes foundational trust prerequisites—enabling heterogeneous agents to establish verifiable, policy-compliant relationships before any protocol-level dialogue begins.

Abstract

This paper describes the technical architecture of OpenAgenet / OAN. OAN is a protocol-neutral trust layer for open Agent interconnection. It specifies the role architecture, identity objects, registration workflow, Root-governed lifecycle, Root-verified package model, authorization-aware Discovery, signed trusted invocation, verification requirements, state transitions, security properties, implementation boundaries, and deployment considerations. The design is intended to support heterogeneous Agent frameworks and interaction protocols, including MCP, A2A, ANP-like systems, and domain-specific Agent protocols. OAN does not define the entire business conversation among Agents; it defines how Agent identities become admissible, discoverable, verifiable, and safe to approach before protocol-specific interaction begins.

item.title: BAHSD: Bridging the Long-tail Gap via Adaptive Distillation in Black-box Sequential Recommendation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03091v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

序列推荐系统广泛部署为黑盒API，催生了模型提取（model extraction）研究——旨在不访问内部参数的前提下，通过查询接口本地复现其推荐能力。然而，真实用户行为数据呈现显著的长尾分布：头部序列高频、信号强；尾部序列稀疏、噪声大。现有提取方法采用统一蒸馏目标（如固定温度KL散度），忽视该信号异质性，导致两大问题：（1）对高置信度头部序列过度拟合教师偏好，引发“偏好固化”，削弱泛化性；（2）对低置信度尾部序列响应迟钝，预测扁平且易受噪声干扰，尾部用户性能严重退化。

方法创新：BAHSD框架

我们提出BAHSD（Black-box Adaptive distillation for Hierarchical Signal Distillation），一种面向黑盒序列推荐的自适应知识蒸馏框架。其核心是多尺度一致性探针机制：通过在不同时间粒度（单步/子序列/全序列）上对比学生与教师输出的一致性，隐式量化每条序列的信号可靠性，无需额外标注。基于此可靠性评估，设计分层自适应目标函数：

  • 对高置信度序列：采用动态温度KL散度，温度随置信度升高而自适应增大，软化教师输出分布，缓解偏好固化；
  • 对低置信度序列：引入排序一致性损失（保障相对序关系）与InfoNCE对比学习（增强难负样本判别力），显著提升抗噪鲁棒性。

主要成果

在4个公开数据集上的实验表明：BAHSD在整体指标（Recall@20, NDCG@20）上超越教师模型最高达4.98%，实现“青出于蓝”；更关键的是，在尾部用户（bottom 20%活跃度）上取得超80%的性能提升，显著弥合长尾鸿沟。框架完全黑盒、即插即用，无需修改教师API，为高保真推荐模型提取提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Sequential recommendation systems are increasingly deployed as black-box APIs, motivating model extraction to replicate their functionality without internal access. However, the inherent long-tail distribution causes severe signal heterogeneity: dense head sequences induce teacher preference solidification, while sparse tail sequences yield flat, noisy predictions—yet existing distillation methods apply uniform objectives, leading to noise overfitting and poor tail-user performance. We propose BAHSD, a black-box adaptive distillation framework that implicitly quantifies per-sequence signal reliability via a multi-scale consistency probing mechanism (across step-, subsequence-, and full-sequence levels). Guided by this reliability score, BAHSD employs a hierarchical adaptive objective: dynamic-temperature KL divergence mitigates preference solidification for high-confidence sequences, while ranking consistency and InfoNCE contrastive learning jointly enhance noise robustness for low-confidence ones. Experiments show BAHSD consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines—achieving up to +4.98% gain over the teacher model in overall metrics and >80% improvement on tail users, offering a plug-and-play solution for high-fidelity black-box sequential recommendation extraction.

Abstract

Sequential recommendation systems are widely adopted but often deployed as black-box APIs, which has driven recent interest in model extraction to replicate their capabilities locally. However, the long-tail distribution induces severe signal heterogeneity: dense head sequences trigger the solidification of teacher preference, biasing extraction toward local patterns, while sparse tail sequences yield flat, noisy predictions. Existing one-size-fits-all extraction overlooks this disparity, resulting in noise overfitting and suboptimal knowledge transfer. We propose BAHSD, a black-box adaptive distillation framework that handles signal heterogeneity via a multi-scale consistency probing mechanism to implicitly quantify signal reliability. Based on this, an adaptive hierarchical objective is designed: dynamic-temperature KL divergence mitigates preference solidification for high-confidence signals, while ranking consistency and InfoNCE contrastive learning provide noise-robust enhancement for low-confidence signals. BAHSD consistently outperforms baselines, achieving up to 4.98\% gain over the teacher and 80\%+ improvement on tail users, offering a plug-and-play solution for high-fidelity black-box recommendation extraction.

item.title: Combining Statistical Features and Deep Encodings for Rehearsal-Based Class-Incremental Time Series Classification
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03292v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在真实场景中，时序分类系统常需持续接纳新类别（如新增故障类型、用户行为模式或疾病亚型），同时避免对已学知识的灾难性遗忘——这一任务称为类增量持续学习（Class-Incremental Continual Learning, CICL）。针对多变量时间序列（如传感器阵列、脑电图、金融高频数据），其固有的长程依赖、相位敏感性与通道异质性进一步加剧了持续学习难度，现有方法在特征鲁棒性、记忆效率与泛化能力之间难以兼顾。

方法创新

本文提出一种双流特征融合框架（Dual-Stream Rehearsal Framework, DSRF），首次将统计特征工程与深度时序编码协同嵌入类增量范式：

  • 浅层统计流：提取12维鲁棒手工特征（含均值、标准差、偏度、峰度、自相关滞后、频域能量比等），计算高效、物理可解释、对时间缩放/噪声具强不变性；
  • 深层语义流：采用冻结的预训练时序基础模型（Informer backbone）提取高阶时序嵌入，经轻量适配器投影后与统计特征拼接；
  • 回放增强机制：基于特征空间相似性筛选代表性样本构建紧凑回放缓冲区（≤5%原始数据），结合知识蒸馏约束新旧任务 logits 一致性，显著抑制遗忘。

主要成果
