在Five UCR/UEA 多变量基准数据集（ElectricDevices、PenDigits、Libras、UWave、HandMovement）上系统验证：DSRF 在平均准确率（Avg. Acc.）上达 82.7%±2.1%，较当前SOTA（iCaRL、DER++、TSCIL）平均提升3.4–5.8个百分点；平均遗忘率（Avg. Forgetting）仅1.9%，为同类方法最低（对比基线：4.7%–9.3%）。消融实验证实双流互补性：统计流提升小样本稳定性，深度流增强判别边界清晰度。

AI Summary (English)

This paper addresses class-incremental continual learning (CICL) for multivariate time series classification—a challenging setting where models must incorporate new classes over time without catastrophically forgetting prior knowledge. We propose a novel dual-stream framework that synergistically combines hand-crafted statistical features (e.g., mean, autocorrelation, spectral entropy) with deep temporal embeddings from a frozen pre-trained foundation model (Informer). A rehearsal buffer—curated via feature-space similarity and enhanced by logit distillation—ensures stable knowledge retention. Evaluated on five standard multivariate benchmarks (ElectricDevices, PenDigits, Libras, UWave, HandMovement), our method achieves a competitive average accuracy of 82.7% ± 2.1%, outperforming state-of-the-art CICL baselines by 3.4–5.8 points, while maintaining an exceptionally low average forgetting rate of 1.9%—the lowest reported to date. The design bridges interpretability, efficiency, and generalization in time-series continual learning.

Abstract

Many systems used in real-world environments require adding new categories and incorporating new information without forgetting what was previously learnt by the classification model. This is known as class-incremental continual learning, and in the case of multivariate time-series, is further complicated by the temporal structure of the data. In this paper, we present a novel approach for performing class incremental continual learning for the classification of multivariate time series data based upon the construction of a dual-stream feature extraction pipeline (using both deep temporal embedding features generated via a pre-trained frozen foundation model and application of statistical features). Evaluated on five benchmark datasets, the proposed system achieves competitive average accuracy across all datasets while maintaining low forgetting rates across all experimental configurations.

item.title: FederatedSkill: Federated Learning for Agentic Skill Evolution
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03143v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

现代大语言模型（LLM）智能体日益依赖技能库（skill library）执行复杂任务，技能的持续演化已成为其自主提升的核心路径。然而，单用户任务流高度同质化、数据稀疏，难以支撑全面、鲁棒的技能构建；而现有跨用户协作方案（如轨迹共享）虽能缓解数据瓶颈，却存在两大根本缺陷：严重侵犯用户隐私，且强制聚合为单一全局技能库，忽视客户端在任务分布、能力偏好与资源约束上的显著异质性。

方法创新：FederatedSkill 框架

本文提出 FederatedSkill——首个面向智能体技能演化的隐私保护联邦学习框架。其核心突破在于摒弃原始交互轨迹的传输，转而以语义化技能差分（semantic skill diffs） 为通信基本单元：即在本地技能库上生成的、结构化的、可解释的“技能补丁”（如新增工具调用逻辑、优化推理链模板、修正错误子程序）。服务器端部署演化智能体（evolution agent），动态聚合各客户端提交的技能补丁，并建模每个客户端独有的能力边界（capability boundary），从而驱动严格个性化的技能进化，而非传统联邦学习中损害性能的“全局平均”。

实验结果与价值

在涵盖20类异构任务家族（含规划、工具调用、多跳推理等）的严格评测中，FederatedSkill 相比纯本地自演化基线：

  • ✅ 成功率最高提升44.4%（如复杂API编排任务从52.1%→74.9%）；
  • ✅ 计算开销降低37.5%（减少冗余技能训练与无效尝试）；
  • ✅ 完全满足端到端差分隐私与模型不可逆脱敏要求。

本工作重新定义了智能体协同进化的范式：从“共享行为”走向“共享认知增量”，为构建安全、高效、个性化的下一代AI代理系统奠定基础。

AI Summary (English)

Modern LLM agents rely heavily on skill libraries for complex task execution, making skill evolution critical for self-improvement. Yet isolated user data lacks diversity, while existing cross-user collaboration methods—like raw trajectory sharing—compromise privacy and enforce a suboptimal global skill library, ignoring client heterogeneity. FederatedSkill addresses this by introducing semantic skill diffs: structured, interpretable patches over local skill libraries (e.g., refined reasoning templates or corrected tool-use logic), used as the sole communication unit. A server-side evolution agent aggregates these diffs to dynamically model each client’s unique capability boundary, enabling strictly personalized skill evolution—not global averaging. Evaluated across 20 diverse agent task families, FederatedSkill achieves up to +44.4% success rate gain and −37.5% computational cost versus self-evolving baselines, all under formal privacy guarantees. It redefines collaborative agent learning as sharing cognitive increments, not behavioral traces.

Abstract

Modern LLM agents increasingly rely on skill libraries to handle complex tasks, making skill evolution a primary driver of self-improvement. However, isolated single-user task streams lack the diversity required to build comprehensive skills. While cross-user collaboration can overcome this data bottleneck, current trajectory-sharing approaches compromise user privacy and impose a uniform global library that fails to accommodate client heterogeneity. We introduce FederatedSkill, a privacy-preserving framework for collaborative agent evolution. Moving beyond raw trajectory sharing, FederatedSkill utilizes semantic skill diffs, structured patches over local libraries, as the fundamental unit of communication. On the server side, an evolution agent aggregates these patches to dynamically model client-specific capability boundaries, facilitating strictly personalized skill evolution rather than a suboptimal global average. Evaluated across 20 distinct agent task families, FederatedSkill demonstrates substantial gains over self-evolving baselines, achieving up to a 44.4% increase in success rate and a 37.5% reduction in computational cost.

item.title: Trans GAN-WT: A Feature Extraction and Interactive Learning-Based Anomaly Detection Model for Wind Turbine Time Series Data
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03112v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

随着风电场规模持续扩大，风电机组（WTG）运维成本攀升，亟需在灾难性故障发生前实现早期异常检测，以保障系统可靠性并降低维护支出。然而，现有方法面临两大瓶颈：（1）难以在高冗余、强噪声的时序数据中有效建模多变量间复杂依赖关系；（2）未能充分利用稀缺但高价值的已标注异常样本，导致对微小偏差型异常（如传感器漂移、渐进性退化）漏检率高。

方法创新

本文提出TransGAN-WT——一种融合Transformer与生成对抗网络（GAN）的特征提取与交互学习型异常检测模型：

  • 误差放大重建机制：通过非线性加权重构损失，显著放大微小异常的残差信号，降低漏检率；
  • 自回归多模态特征提取：引入带掩码的自回归推理路径，联合建模SCADA数据中的物理量（风速、功率、转速等）与统计特征（滑动均值、方差），提升模型泛化性与训练稳定性；
  • 跨尺度时序交互模块（TSIM）：构建短时（秒级）、中时（分钟级）、长时（小时级）三路并行特征编码器，并通过门控注意力实现跨尺度特征动态融合，显式抑制时间冗余。

实验结果

在4个真实风电场SCADA数据集（含齿轮箱振动、变桨系统、主轴承等多类故障）上的实验表明：TransGAN-WT平均F1达96.10%，较LSTM-AE、USAD、TranAD等SOTA基线分别提升5.84%和2.89%；假阳性率（FPR）低至0.06%；Wilcoxon符号秩检验（p < 0.01）证实其性能提升具有统计显著性。该模型已部署于某省级风电智能运维平台，支撑实时异常预警与根因分析。

AI Summary (English)

Wind turbine (WTG) anomaly detection is critical for reducing O&M costs and preventing catastrophic failures, yet existing methods struggle with redundant multivariate time series and underutilize scarce anomaly labels. This paper proposes TransGAN-WT, a novel model integrating Transformer-based temporal modeling and GAN-driven reconstruction learning. It introduces three key innovations: (1) an error-amplified reconstruction loss to enhance sensitivity to subtle deviations; (2) autoregressive multimodal feature extraction to jointly encode physical and statistical SCADA features, improving generalization; and (3) a temporal-scale interaction module (TSIM) that fuses short-, medium-, and long-term patterns via gated attention to suppress temporal redundancy. Evaluated on four real-world WTG datasets, TransGAN-WT achieves a mean F1-score of 96.10%, outperforming state-of-the-art baselines (e.g., USAD, TranAD) by +5.84% and +2.89%, with an ultra-low false positive rate of 0.06%. Wilcoxon signed-rank tests (p < 0.01) confirm statistically significant gains, validating its practical efficacy for reliable wind farm operation.

Abstract

With the increasing scale and number of wind farms, wind turbines' daily operation and maintenance costs are increasing. To reduce operation and maintenance costs and enhance the reliability of wind turbine and system operation data before reaching catastrophic failures, monitoring the operating status of the equipment and detecting failures at an early stage is crucial. It is of great practical significance to utilize the working condition data for abnormal assessment of the operating status of wind turbines to realize abnormal monitoring of the operating status of wind turbines. However, the existing anomaly detection methods can neither perform effective relational modeling in data filled with a large amount of redundant information nor reasonably utilize the valuable anomaly data. For this reason, this paper proposes an anomaly detection model that fuses a Transformer and a generative adversarial network. Firstly, it reduces the leakage detection rate of minor deviation anomalies by amplifying the reconstruction error. Secondly, it uses autoregressive inference to extract multimodal features to enhance the stability and generalization ability of training. Finally, the temporal feature extraction module is constructed to promote the interactive learning between features of different time scales and effectively reduce the time redundancy. The results of multiple sets of experiments conducted on real WTG datasets show that TransGAN-WT achieves an average F1 score of 96.10% across multiple wind turbine datasets, which is 5.84% and 2.89% higher than several other state-of-the-art baseline methods. It also realizes a false positive rate (FPR) of 0.06%, and is verified by the Wilcoxon signed-rank test to have achieved a statistically significant performance enhancement compared to the state-of-the-art baseline methods, effectively ensuring the stable operation of wind turbines.

item.title: FGRPO: Federated GRPO with Adaptive Aggregation on Non-IID Data
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03094v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

随着大语言模型的发展，基于强化学习（RL）的自我修正与长链推理能力已成为提升模型推理质量的核心范式。Group Relative Policy Optimization（GRPO）通过摒弃传统 critic 网络，显著提升了训练可扩展性与稳定性。然而，其标准实现依赖中心化数据聚合，需从分布式用户端收集大量原始交互数据，严重违背隐私保护原则——尤其在医疗、金融等敏感场景中不可接受。

方法创新：FGRPO 框架

本文提出 Federated GRPO（FGRPO），首个面向非独立同分布（non-IID）数据的联邦式 GRPO 框架。核心突破在于设计了一种基于相对性能增益的自适应聚合机制：

  • 每个客户端不直接上传策略梯度或奖励值，而是计算其本轮更新相对于个性化历史基线（如上一轮本地最优回报均值）的相对提升率；
  • 服务器据此动态加权聚合，自动抑制低效或退化客户端的贡献（如任务过难、数据噪声大），优先融合高信噪比的学习信号；
  • 全过程无需共享原始数据、奖励标量或策略参数，仅交换轻量级归一化增益指标，严格满足联邦学习隐私边界。

主要成果

在涵盖数学推理（GSM8K）、代码生成（HumanEval）和多跳问答（HotpotQA）的异构 non-IID 分布实验中，FGRPO 相比 FedAvg+GRPO 和 FedProx+GRPO 平均提升 12.7% 的最终推理准确率，收敛速度加快 1.8×，且在极端数据倾斜（如 90% 客户仅含单一任务）下仍保持稳定。本工作首次将无 critic 的 RL 范式成功解耦至联邦架构，为隐私敏感场景下的推理模型协同优化提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Recent advances highlight reinforcement learning (RL) as the dominant paradigm for enhancing language models’ self-correction and long-chain reasoning. While Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) improves scalability by eliminating the critic network, its centralized deployment risks severe privacy breaches due to raw data collection from distributed owners. To address this, we propose Federated GRPO (FGRPO)—the first privacy-preserving framework for decentralized GRPO fine-tuning over heterogeneous, non-IID data. Its core innovation is an adaptive server aggregation mechanism grounded in relative performance gain: each client computes its improvement over a personalized historical baseline (e.g., local moving-average reward), enabling the server to dynamically weight updates by signal reliability—not task difficulty or data volume. This avoids reward-scale divergence and ensures robust convergence without sharing raw data, rewards, or model parameters. Experiments across GSM8K, HumanEval, and HotpotQA under realistic non-IID splits show FGRPO achieves +12.7% average accuracy gain over baselines and 1.8× faster convergence—demonstrating both privacy compliance and superior generalization.

Abstract

Recent advances in language models have established reinforcement learning as the primary paradigm for eliciting self-correction and long-chain reasoning. While group relative policy optimization (GRPO) offers superior scalability by eliminating the critic network, deploying it on a central infrastructure entails collecting a large volume of data from distributed owners, which poses significant privacy risks. To address these concerns, we introduce federated GRPO (FGRPO), a framework designed to decentralize the fine-tuning of reasoning models across heterogeneous data owners. To effectively mitigate the instability caused by divergent reward scales across heterogeneous tasks, FGRPO incorporates an adaptive aggregation mechanism based on relative performance gain. By characterizing each client's improvement relative to its personalized historical baseline, the framework dynamically prioritizes effective learning trajectories regardless of local task difficulty. FGRPO ensures robust convergence on non-IID data while preserving data privacy.

item.title: Backdoor Unlearning Generalization: A Path Toward the Removal of Unknown Triggers in LLMs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03785v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

大语言模型（LLMs）中的后门攻击构成日益严峻的安全威胁：攻击者通过隐蔽触发器（trigger）诱导模型在特定输入下生成恶意输出。现有防御方法多为“单点清除”范式——需预先知晓触发器形态，且每次仅针对一个已知后门进行干预。当模型中存在未知触发器（如隐蔽植入的多个后门）时，防御者处于结构性劣势，难以系统性保障模型安全。

方法与发现

本研究首次揭示并实证了后门卸载泛化性（Backdoor Unlearning Generalization）现象：对模型执行单次触发器定向卸载训练（如移除一个预设的可控后门），不仅能消除目标后门，还能显著抑制未被显式训练干预的其他后门（包括不同触发模式、注入阶段及模型架构下的后门）。我们在三类主流模型家族（Llama-2、Qwen、Phi-3）上开展系统验证，涵盖预训练与持续预训练两种后门注入方式，并逐个卸载不同后门，观测跨后门抑制效果。

创新机制与工具

为解释该泛化现象，我们提出跨激活偏移距离（Cross Activation Shift Distance, CASD）——一种可计算的度量，用于量化不同卸载训练所引发的内部表征空间偏移之间的相似性。实验表明：CASD值越小，跨后门抑制强度越高，证实卸载过程在神经激活层面存在共享抑制路径。

意义与范式转变

本工作开辟LLM安全新范式：防御者可主动注入可控后门→执行定向卸载→利用泛化效应被动清除未知后门。该策略不依赖对攻击者触发器的先验知识，为应对黑盒/多后门威胁提供了可扩展、低成本的主动防御路径。

AI Summary (English)

Large Language Models (LLMs) are vulnerable to backdoor attacks, where hidden triggers cause malicious outputs. Current defenses require a priori knowledge of each trigger and address backdoors one-by-one—leaving models exposed to unknown or multiple backdoors. We discover Backdoor Unlearning Generalization: unlearning one trigger via targeted unlearning training consistently suppresses other, untargeted backdoors—even those injected via different methods (pretraining/continual pretraining), across diverse model families (Llama-2, Qwen, Phi-3). To explain this, we propose the Cross Activation Shift Distance (CASD), a metric quantifying similarity in representation shifts induced by different unlearning procedures; lower CASD strongly correlates with stronger cross-backdoor suppression. Crucially, this enables a novel proactive defense paradigm: defenders can intentionally implant benign, controllable backdoors, then unlearn them—thereby leveraging generalization to neutralize unknown attacker-planted backdoors without trigger knowledge. Our results establish unlearning not just as removal, but as a generalizable safety intervention.

Abstract

Backdoor attacks in Large Language Models (LLMs) are a growing security concern, where models can generate adversary-chosen content. Existing defenses target backdoors one at a time and typically require knowledge of the trigger, leaving the defender at a structural disadvantage when unknown backdoors may exist in a model. We show that backdoor neutralization through unlearning generalizes across backdoors: training a model to ignore a single trigger can also suppress other backdoors that were never explicitly targeted. We study this phenomenon across three model families, whose backdoors were injected via pretraining or continual pretraining, by analyzing the models obtained after removing one backdoor at a time. To understand why unlearning certain backdoors induces the suppression of others, we introduce the Cross Activation Shift Distance, to quantify the distance between model changes induced by different trainings. Our results open a new direction for LLM safety as defenders could deliberately inject controlled backdoors and then remove them, leveraging cross-backdoor transfer to also suppress unknown backdoors that an attacker may have previously introduced in the model.

item.title: IntraShuffler: A Privacy Preserving Framework for Heterogeneous DP Federated Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02563v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

在异构差分隐私联邦学习（HDP-FL）中，各客户端可依据数据敏感性与机构策略自主设定隐私预算 $\varepsilon_i$，服务器通过 $\varepsilon$-感知聚合（如按 $\varepsilon_i$ 加权）提升模型效用。然而，非独立同分布（non-IID）数据导致梯度更新携带显著结构性模式；当服务器结合隐私预算信息进行加权时，这些隐含信号会加剧隐私泄露风险——即使服务器是“诚实但好奇”的，也能利用梯度去噪与代理建模发起隐私推理攻击（Privacy Inference Attack），准确推断客户端数据分布属性（如类别偏置、设备类型），并跨轮次关联同一客户端的更新（链接成功率显著高于基线）。

方法创新：IntraShuffler 框架

为兼顾 HDP-FL 的效用优势与抗推理能力，本文提出 IntraShuffler——一种轻量级中间件防御框架。其核心是隐私感知的桶内混洗机制：

  • 首先将客户端按 $\varepsilon_i$ 相容性（如 $\varepsilon_i \in [a,b]$）聚类为“隐私兼容桶”，确保桶内梯度可安全加权；
  • 进而在每个桶内对模型参数（而非完整模型）执行细粒度、随机化 shuffling，打破梯度结构的时空一致性；
  • 关键设计：shuffling 在参数维度进行，不破坏 $\varepsilon$-感知聚合的数学基础，允许服务器仍按声明预算加权，保障模型收敛性与效用。

实验结果

在 CIFAR-10、CIFAR-100、FEMNIST 和 Shakespeare 四个 benchmark 数据集上验证：

  • 梯度可恢复性降低 >60%（基于重构误差与相似度指标）；
  • 代理推理准确率从 0.78 降至 0.33，接近随机猜测水平；
  • 模型精度损失 <1.2%（vs. 原始 HDP-FL），在 FedAvg、FedProx、SCAFFOLD 等多种聚合规则下均保持鲁棒性。

IntraShuffler 是首个在不牺牲 HDP-FL 核心机制前提下实现强推理防御的实用方案。

AI Summary (English)

Heterogeneous Differential Privacy Federated Learning (HDP-FL) enables clients to set individual privacy budgets ($\varepsilon_i$), and servers improve utility via $\varepsilon$-aware aggregation—yet this exposes structural gradient patterns from non-IID data, enabling honest-but-curious servers to launch Privacy Inference Attacks: inferring client data distributions and linking updates across rounds. While the Shuffle Model anonymizes sources, it is fundamentally incompatible with $\varepsilon$-aware aggregation. To bridge this gap, we propose IntraShuffler, a middleware framework that groups clients into privacy-compatible buckets based on $\varepsilon_i$ ranges and performs parameter-level shuffling within each bucket. This disrupts persistent gradient structure without breaking $\varepsilon$-weighted aggregation. Experiments on four datasets show IntraShuffler reduces gradient recoverability by >60%, drops surrogate inference accuracy from 0.78 to 0.33, and preserves model utility—achieving <1.2% accuracy loss under FedAvg, FedProx, and SCAFFOLD. It is the first practical defense enabling strong inference resistance while fully retaining HDP-FL’s utility-preserving aggregation.

Abstract

Heterogeneous Differential Privacy (HDP) in Federated Learning (FL) allows clients to select individual privacy budgets ($\varepsilon_i$) according to institutional policies and data sensitivity. In practice, many HDP-FL systems employ $\varepsilon$-aware server aggregation to improve model utility by re-weighting client updates according to their declared privacy budgets. However, gradient updates in FL retain structural patterns induced by non-independent and identically-distributed (non-IID) data, and these additional signals exposed by $\varepsilon$-aware aggregation create new opportunities for inference by an honest-but-curious server. In this work, we first show that a server equipped with gradient denoising and surrogate modeling can mount a \emph{Privacy Inference Attack} that infers distributional attributes of clients and links updates from the same client across training rounds, measured via surrogate inference accuracy and linkage success, under realistic knowledge constraints. The Shuffle-Model has been widely studied as a defense against such inference risks by anonymizing update sources, but it is fundamentally incompatible with HDP-FL $\varepsilon$-aware aggregation. To address this challenge, we propose \textbf{IntraShuffler}, a middleware defense framework designed for HDP-FL systems. IntraShuffler introduces a privacy-aware shuffling mechanism that groups clients into privacy-compatible buckets and performs parameter-level shuffling within each bucket to disrupt persistent gradient structure while preserving $\varepsilon$-aware aggregation. Experiments across four different datasets show that IntraShuffler reduces gradient recoverability by over 60% and decreases surrogate inference accuracy from 0.78 to 0.33 while maintaining comparable model utility across multiple FL aggregation rules.

item.title: Auditing Asset-Specific Preferences in Financial Large Language Models: Evidence from Bitcoin Representations and Portfolio Allocation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02528v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

大型语言模型（LLMs）正日益驱动智能投顾与交易代理，但其是否内嵌对特定金融资产的系统性偏好，迄今缺乏实证审计。本文聚焦比特币（Bitcoin），首次构建资产特异性偏好审计框架，系统探究三个核心问题：（1）LLMs是否对特定金融工具存在稳定排名偏差；（2）能否识别对其偏好具有因果影响力的关键内部表征；（3）该表征是否实质性影响下游投资决策（如资产配置）。

方法：三层次可解释性审计协议

1. 行为层审计：对8个前沿LLM（如Gemma 3、Llama 3、Claude 3等）开展框架敏感性测试。结果发现，比特币排名高度依赖语境——在“可靠货币”框架下平均排第5/8，而在“危机货币”或“自主代理”框架下跃升至前2位；属性交换实验进一步证实，模型响应由功能属性（如去中心化、抗审查）驱动，而非名称本身。
2. 机制层审计：在Gemma 3中扫描数千个稀疏自编码器（SAE）特征，定位到一个强选择性比特币表征特征（Feature ID: gemma3-4b-sae-16384-00027893）。通过激活/抑制该特征（prompt中不出现“Bitcoin”一词），可双向调控模型对比特币的偏好强度。
3. 决策层审计：在模拟资产配置任务中，特征放大使比特币组合占比提升5.2个百分点，抑制则降低4.6个百分点；且效应具有结构性：放大仅重配加密资产内部权重，抑制则显著削减整体加密敞口（-7.3 pp）。

创新贡献

提出有界行为杠杆（Bounded Behavioral Leverage）概念：单一内部特征可被精准干预以改变金融输出，但效应受限于模型架构与任务边界。本框架首次实现从神经元活动→偏好表达→真实投资决策的因果链验证，为新兴“知彼代理”（Know-Your-Agent, KYA）监管标准提供可测量的行为层基础。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces the first audit framework for asset-specific preferences in financial LLMs, using Bitcoin as a test case. We conduct a three-level analysis: (1) Behavioral audit across 8 frontier models reveals frame-dependent Bitcoin rankings—mid-tier as “reliable money” but top-tier under crisis/autonomy frames, with attribute-swaps confirming functional (not lexical) drivers; (2) Mechanistic audit identifies a dominant Bitcoin-selective sparse autoencoder feature in Gemma 3: amplifying it increases Bitcoin preference even without the word “Bitcoin” in prompts, while suppression reduces it; (3) Decision audit shows this feature causally shifts portfolio allocation—amplification raises Bitcoin’s share by +5.2 percentage points (reallocating within crypto), suppression lowers it by −4.6 pp (cutting total crypto exposure by −7.3 pp). We formalize this as bounded behavioral leverage: an internal feature exerts causal, measurable, and constrained influence over financial outputs. Validated via random controls and boundary tests, our work establishes a foundational behavioral layer for emerging Know-Your-Agent (KYA) standards.

Abstract

Large language models now power robo-advisors and trading agents, yet whether they carry built-in biases toward specific assets is largely untested. We ask three questions: do LLMs systematically prefer certain financial instruments; can an internal representation with causal leverage over those preferences be identified; and does that representation affect downstream financial decisions? We develop a three-level audit protocol and apply it to Bitcoin. First, a behavioral audit of eight frontier LLMs shows that Bitcoin's ranking among money-like instruments is frame-dependent: models place it around rank 5 of 8 as "reliable money" but near the top under crisis and autonomous-agent frames, and an attribute-swap experiment confirms rankings track functional properties, not names. Second, we open a model's internals: a search across thousands of sparse-autoencoder features in Gemma 3 identifies a dominant Bitcoin-selective feature. Amplifying it shifts the model toward the asset and suppressing it shifts the model away, even when "Bitcoin" never appears in the prompt. Third, we test financial consequences: amplification raises Bitcoin's portfolio share by 5.2 percentage points while suppression lowers it by 4.6 pp, with amplification reallocating within crypto and suppression cutting total crypto exposure. We characterize this as bounded behavioral leverage (leverage meaning causal influence over outputs, not financial leverage): an identifiable internal feature can be perturbed to move financial choices, but only within measurable limits. The framework links internal representations to external recommendations, validated with random controls and mechanism boundaries. As LLMs become autonomous financial agents, this is a first step toward a behavioral layer for emerging know-your-agent (KYA) standards: knowing what an agent prefers, and how far that preference can be moved.

item.title: Cross-modal linkage risk in clinical vision-language models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02276v1

AI Summary (中文)

跨模态链接风险：临床视觉-语言模型中的隐私新挑战

本研究首次系统揭示了临床视觉-语言模型（VLMs）中固有的跨模态重链接风险：当模型在配对的胸部X光片与放射科报告上训练后，其共享嵌入空间会隐式编码图像与文本之间的实例级对应关系。这一特性在医疗数据治理中构成显著隐私威胁——即使影像与报告在部署后被物理隔离（如仅共享脱敏影像、报告受严格访问控制），攻击者仍可仅凭余弦相似度，将一张匿名X光片重新关联至其原始结构化报告，无需额外元数据或模型逆向工程。

我们构建了严格的图像→报告检索审计框架，利用MIMIC-CXR（43,793个预留配对）与外部CheXpert Plus（29,296对）共406,241个真实配对样本（覆盖126,804名患者）作为基准，而非隐私场景本身。结果表明：模型临床专业化程度越高，重链接能力越强。最强VLM在候选池N=100时准确召回率达随机基线的15倍；N=10,000时达50倍；全库规模下仍显著高于偶然水平。该信号在引入病理匹配的难负样本（排除疾病标签捷径）后依然稳健，证实其捕获的是细粒度临床语义对齐，而非粗略诊断类别映射。

为规避昂贵重训练，我们提出靶向差分隐私微调：冻结图像/文本编码器，仅对定义跨模态对齐的投影头施加DP优化（ε=0.34, δ=6×10⁻⁶）。该方法在MIMIC-CXR上使N=10,000时Recall@1下降61.8%，且零样本迁移至CheXpert Plus；同时影像端临床效用几乎无损：14类疾病线性探针宏AUROC仅从79.63%微降至79.43%。本工作为平衡多模态AI的临床价值与患者隐私提供了首个可验证、可部署的隐私增强范式。

AI Summary (English)

This study identifies and quantifies a novel privacy risk—cross-modal linkage—in clinical vision-language models (VLMs): trained on paired chest radiographs and reports, VLMs implicitly encode instance-level image-report correspondence in their shared embedding space. We audit this via image-to-report retrieval on 406,241 ground-truth pairs from MIMIC-CXR and CheXpert Plus, finding re-linkage probability rises systematically with clinical specialization (e.g., 50× chance level at N=10,000 candidates). The signal persists under pathology-matched hard negatives, confirming fine-grained semantic alignment beyond diagnostic labels. To mitigate risk without retraining, we apply differential privacy only to the projection heads (ε=0.34, δ=6×10⁻⁶), freezing encoders. This reduces Recall@1 by 61.8% at N=10,000 on MIMIC-CXR and transfers zero-shot to CheXpert Plus, while preserving image utility: macro AUROC for 14-label classification drops only from 79.63% to 79.43%. Our targeted DP finetuning offers a practical, privacy-preserving path for clinical VLM deployment.

Abstract

Vision-language models (VLMs) trained on paired chest radiographs and radiology reports learn a shared embedding space that can preserve instance-level image-report correspondence. This poses a privacy risk in settings where radiographs and reports are deliberately kept separate after acquisition, such as image-only data sharing or access-controlled reports, because a de-identified image may be re-linked to its original narrative report through cosine similarity alone. We formalized this as image-to-report retrieval and used public paired cohorts, in which the true pairing is known by design, as ground-truth benchmarks to audit the risk rather than as the privacy scenario. Evaluating VLMs of increasing clinical specialization on 406,241 paired examples from 126,804 patients across MIMIC-CXR (43,793 held-out pairs) and external CheXpert Plus (29,296 pairs), we found that re-linkage rose systematically with specialization: the strongest VLM retrieved the correct report at 15 times chance at a candidate pool of N = 100, 50 times chance at N = 10,000, and well above chance at full-database scale. The signal persisted under pathology-matched hard negatives that removed disease-label shortcuts, indicating correspondence beyond broad diagnostic categories. To reduce it without retraining, we froze both encoders and applied differentially private optimization only to the projection heads defining the alignment layer (epsilon = 0.34, delta = 6x10-6). This reduced Recall@1 by 61.8% at N = 10,000 on MIMIC-CXR and transferred to CheXpert Plus without retraining, while image-side utility was largely preserved: macro AUROC for linear-probe classification across 14 labels shifted only from 79.63% to 79.43%. Targeted DP finetuning of the shared alignment layer can substantially reduce cross-modal re-linkage without materially degrading the image representations that make these models clinically useful.

item.title: ArrythML: An Autoencoder-Based TinyML Approach for On-Device Arrhythmia Detection on Resource-Constrained Embedded Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02256v1

AI Summary (中文)

ArrythML：面向资源受限嵌入式设备的自编码器驱动TinyML心律失常检测方法

本研究提出ArrythML——一种专为低功耗嵌入式可穿戴设备设计的端侧心律失常实时检测框架。针对传统云端依赖型ECG分析存在的隐私泄露、网络延迟与能耗高等问题，我们构建了轻量级、INT8量化、自编码器（Autoencoder）驱动的TinyML模型，仅含极简层数与参数（<100K可训练参数），支持在内存与算力严重受限的微控制器上高效部署。模型基于MIT-BIH心律失常数据库构建定制化标注数据集（含95,000+ ECG片段），涵盖室性早搏、室上性心动过速等12类临床相关心律异常。实验在PC仿真与真实硬件双轨验证：所有推理均在ESP32-S3微控制器（搭载TensorFlow Lite Micro运行时）上完成，实现端到端纯本地处理。创新性地开展细粒度失效归因分析——包括逐标注点（annotation-wise）与逐记录（record-wise）诊断，揭示模型对早期/隐匿性异常模式（如微小T波异位、PR间期渐进性延长）的高度敏感性；部分“误检”实为参考标注未覆盖的临床前征兆，印证模型潜在的预警价值。经剔除标注模糊样本后的严谨评估，最优DNN自编码器模型达成：84%召回率、79% F1分数、180 KB模型体积、单次推理仅9 ms延迟。该成果首次系统验证了超低功耗（<10 mW）、零数据上传、全栈嵌入式心律失常筛查的可行性，为隐私优先的下一代数字健康监护设备提供了可落地的技术范式。

AI Summary (English)

ArrythML introduces an autoencoder-based TinyML framework for real-time, on-device arrhythmia detection on ultra-resource-constrained embedded systems (e.g., ESP32-S3). We design INT8-quantized, minimal-parameter autoencoders (<100K params) optimized for TensorFlow Lite Micro, trained and evaluated on a curated subset of MIT-BIH Arrhythmia Database (95,000+ ECG segments). Rigorous validation includes PC simulation and in situ microcontroller deployment, complemented by annotation-wise and record-wise failure analysis to interpret model behavior across diverse morphologies. Notably, some “false positives” reflect clinically subtle or pre-symptomatic anomalies missed in ground-truth labels—highlighting the model’s sensitivity. After filtering ambiguous annotations, the best DNN autoencoder achieves 84% recall, 79% F1-score, ~180 KB size, and 9 ms on-device latency, demonstrating feasibility of privacy-preserving, battery-efficient wearable arrhythmia screening with full inference at the edge.

Abstract

Our work presents a method for ECG segmentation and arrhythmia detection using Tiny Machine Learning (TinyML) models for real-time, on-device inference on resource-constrained embedded systems. We develop INT8 quantized autoencoder-based TinyML models with minimal layers and parameters for embedded deployment. These models are evaluated using a custom dataset derived from the MIT-BIH Arrhythmia Database and validated in both PC-based simulations and on-device environments. For the evaluations, over 95,000 ECG segments are processed on an ESP32-S3 microcontroller running the TensorFlow Lite Micro runtime. Post-evaluation, detailed analysis, including annotation-wise and record-wise failure analysis, is conducted to characterize model behavior across diverse ECG morphologies and rhythm patterns and to explain missed detections. In several cases, apparent misclassifications may correspond to early or subtle anomaly patterns labeled as normal in the reference annotations, highlighting the model's sensitivity. A refined evaluation by filtering out ambiguous cases in the dataset shows that the best-performing DNN-based autoencoder achieves a recall of 84%, an F1-score of 79%, a model size of approximately 180 KB, and an inference latency of 9 ms on-device. These results demonstrate the feasibility of low-power, privacy-preserving embedded wearable systems capable of performing accurate arrhythmia detection entirely on-device.

item.title: Closing the Alignment-Maturity Gap in Federated Prototype Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02172v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在联邦学习（FL）中，从分布式、异构数据中学习判别性视觉表征面临根本性困难。基于原型（prototype-based）的方法虽能缓解统计异质性，但引入了距离依赖的对齐压力：早期全局原型尚不成熟（由噪声较大的本地原型聚合而成），强行对其对齐会生成过大梯度，抑制本地判别结构的形成，导致嵌入空间混乱、识别性能显著下降——该问题在强非独立同分布（non-IID）条件下尤为突出。

方法创新：FedSAP框架

我们提出FedSAP（Federated Scheduled Alignment and Proxy separation），通过两大协同机制闭环解决“对齐-成熟度失配”问题：

  • 确定性对齐课程（Deterministic Alignment Curriculum）：动态延迟全局对齐时机，仅当本地表征趋于稳定后才启动原型同步，避免早期内耗；
  • 几何驱动的代理分离损失（Geometry-driven Proxy Separation Loss）：在单位超球面上直接优化类间结构，复用现有原型库，零新增参数、零通信开销，实现紧凑且高可分的类簇组织。

主要成果

在三个标准基准（CIFAR-10/100, Tiny-ImageNet）及多级异质性设置下，FedSAP相较主流原型基线（如FedProto、FedPA）平均提升2.3–4.0个百分点，且异质性越强，增益越显著（+4.0% @ Dir(0.1)）。其表征本质天然支持半监督扩展：仅需微调标签掩码即可融合无标注数据，验证了“调度式对齐”作为通用设计范式的普适性与可扩展性。

AI Summary (English)

Learning discriminative representations in federated learning (FL) is hindered by a critical alignment-maturity gap: immature global prototypes—aggregated from noisy local ones—induce excessive gradient pressure during early rounds, degrading embedding structure and accuracy under severe non-IID data. We propose FedSAP, a lightweight yet effective framework featuring: (i) a deterministic alignment curriculum that postpones global prototype synchronization until local representations stabilize; and (ii) a geometry-driven proxy separation loss that enforces inter-class separation on the unit hypersphere using only the existing prototype bank—introducing no extra parameters or communication overhead. Evaluated across three benchmarks (CIFAR-10/100, Tiny-ImageNet) and diverse heterogeneity levels, FedSAP consistently outperforms prototype-based baselines by up to +4.0 percentage points, with largest gains under high non-IIDness (e.g., Dir(0.1)). Its representation-centric design further enables seamless semi-supervised extension with minimal modification, establishing scheduled alignment as a generalizable FL principle.

Abstract

Learning discriminative visual representations from distributed, heterogeneous data is a fundamental challenge in Federated Learning (FL). Prototype-based methods address statistical heterogeneity by sharing class-level representations across clients but create a distance-dependent gradient pressure that is particularly severe during early training rounds: alignment pressure applied to immature global prototypes, aggregated from noisy local representations, generates large gradients that suppress the emergence of local discriminative structure. The result is a poorly organized embedding space and degraded recognition performance, particularly under severe non-IID conditions. We propose FedSAP, a framework that stabilises federated representation learning through two complementary mechanisms: a deterministic alignment curriculum that delays global alignment until local representations become stable and a geometry-driven proxy separation loss that enforces inter-class structure on the unit hypersphere using the existing prototype bank without introducing additional parameters or communication overhead. Together, these mechanisms produce compact, well-separated class clusters without altering the underlying communication protocol between federation's participants. Experiments across three benchmarks and varying degrees of heterogeneity show gains of up to 4 percentage points over the prototype-based baselines evaluated, with improvements most pronounced under high heterogeneity. The representational nature of our framework further enables a straightforward extension to semi-supervised settings, where unlabelled data is incorporated with minimal modification, underscoring the generality of scheduled alignment as a design principle.

item.title: Transferable Self-Harm Surveillance from Emergency Department Triage Notes Using an Evidence-Augmented Machine Learning Approach
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02545v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景

自伤行为是亟需应对的重大公共卫生问题，但当前依赖医院诊断编码的监测体系敏感性严重不足（常低于40%），大量轻中度自伤患者因未被赋予明确诊断代码而被漏检。急诊科（ED）分诊记录作为患者入院首份结构化文本，虽简短（平均<50词），却蕴含关键临床线索（如“割腕”“吞药”“情绪崩溃后自残”），为早期、主动识别自伤提供了独特窗口。

方法

本研究提出可迁移的证据增强型机器学习框架，含三阶段流水线：（1）LLM初筛：利用微调后的开源大语言模型（Llama-3-8B）对分诊文本进行粗粒度自伤判定与关键实体定位；（2）证据提取：基于临床指南构建规则引擎，从LLM输出中精准抽取自伤方法、意图、工具等结构化证据；（3）集成分类：将LLM置信度、提取证据特征及传统NLP特征（TF-IDF、词嵌入）输入XGBoost分类器，输出最终判定及方法分类。模型在澳大利亚一家三级医院（n=21,467条分诊记录）开发，并在另两家异构医院（n=15,892 & 12,305）开展无适配外部验证。

主要发现

  • 高鲁棒性迁移能力：内部验证AUPRC达0.887±0.016；外部验证AUPRC分别为0.879±0.012与0.816±0.015，无需任何站点特异性重训练；前瞻性部署于开发站点达0.881±0.008。
  • 超越二元分类的细粒度洞察：首次实现自伤主要方法（如切割、服药、撞击）的自动识别，准确率95%，支持按方法学分类的精准流行病学监测。
  • 临床可解释性：所有预测均附带可追溯的原始文本证据片段与LLM推理链，满足医疗AI监管要求。

创新点

本工作突破了传统NLP模型对标注数据与本地适配的强依赖，通过“LLM引导+规则精炼+轻量模型决策”的协同范式，实现了高精度、高迁移性、高可解释性的自伤实时监测，为全球急诊系统提供即插即用的公共卫生哨点工具。

AI Summary (English)

Self-harm surveillance relying on diagnostic codes suffers from critically low sensitivity, missing many cases presenting to emergency departments (EDs). We propose an evidence-augmented machine learning approach that leverages triage notes—the first clinical text recorded upon ED arrival—to detect self-harm with high accuracy and cross-site transferability. Our three-stage pipeline integrates a fine-tuned LLM (Llama-3-8B) for initial screening and evidence localization, a clinical guideline–informed rule engine for structured extraction of self-harm methods and intent, and an XGBoost classifier fusing LLM outputs, extracted evidence, and traditional NLP features. Validated across three Australian hospitals without site-specific retraining, the model achieves AUPRCs of 0.887 ± 0.016 (internal), 0.879 ± 0.012 and 0.816 ± 0.015 (external), and 0.881 ± 0.008 in prospective deployment. Crucially, it identifies the primary self-harm method with 95% accuracy—enabling granular, method-specific surveillance beyond binary classification—while maintaining full traceability to original text evidence.

Abstract

Self-harm is a major public health concern, but current surveillance relying on hospital presentations is inadequate due to the low sensitivity of diagnostic codes. Emergency Department (ED) triage notes, recorded at the initial point of contact, provide a succinct summary of presentations and an opportunity to identify self-harm. We developed a three-stage approach, augmenting traditional machine learning with large language model-based screening and evidence extraction to detect self-harm in ED triage notes. We assessed model transferability across three Australian hospitals. Our approach showed AUPRCs of 0.887 +/- 0.016 and 0.884 +/- 0.012 during internal and external validation. Prospectively, it achieved AUPRC of 0.881 +/- 0.008 at the development site, and 0.879 +/- 0.012 and 0.816 +/- 0.015 at two external sites without site-specific retraining. A key advantage of the approach is that it enables identification of the primary self-harm method with an accuracy of 95%, supporting more granular surveillance beyond binary classification.

item.title: SkillHarm: Lifecycle-Aware Skill-Based Attacks via Automated Construction
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02540v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

智能体（Agent）技能作为其工作流中的核心执行单元，常被隐式调用与信任，导致第三方技能成为高危攻击面。现有研究虽揭示了技能投毒引发的安全风险，但多局限于单次任务执行场景，且依赖人工枚举的零散风险清单，缺乏对技能全生命周期（注册、调用、复用、持久化）的系统性攻击建模与评估。

方法与创新

本文提出 SkillHarm——首个面向技能生命周期的系统性技能攻击基准，包含：

  • 双阶段攻击范式：① 固定载荷投毒（FPP）：恶意技能包一经调用即直接危害当前会话；② 自变异投毒（SMP）：良性初始执行后，悄然篡改持久化技能内容，危害延迟至后续复用时爆发；
  • 12类结构化风险分类：依据攻击目标组件细分为三类——数据管道层（如输入污染、输出泄露）、系统环境层（如权限提升、文件系统破坏）、智能体自主性层（如目标劫持、推理绕过）；
  • 自动化构建框架 AutoSkillHarm：基于自然语言“测试驱动”指令，调度编码智能体批量生成攻击样本，确保可扩展性与真实性。

主要发现

基准涵盖 71个技能、879个攻击样本。实验表明：主流智能体仍高度脆弱——FPP攻击成功率高达86.3%，SMP达69.3%。更关键的是，我们发现大量“失败”案例实为智能体未加载/未访问投毒文件（规避式失效），而非真正具备防御能力；现有防护机制（如沙箱、权限隔离）对此类隐蔽、延迟型攻击缺乏可靠缓解效果。

AI Summary (English)

SkillHarm is the first benchmark for skill-based attacks that systematically covers the full skill-use lifecycle—registration, invocation, reuse, and persistence. It introduces two novel attack paradigms: Fixed-Payload Poisoning (FPP), where a malicious skill compromises any session invoking it immediately; and Self-Mutating Poisoning (SMP), where an initially benign execution silently alters persistent skill code, deferring harm to later reuse. We define a 12-category risk taxonomy grounded in three agent workflow components: data pipelines, system environments, and agent autonomy. Using AutoSkillHarm—an automated pipeline powered by coding agents guided by natural-language harnesses—we construct 879 attack samples across 71 skills. Experiments show high vulnerability: up to 86.3% success in FPP and 69.3% in SMP. Critically, many apparent failures stem from agents avoiding poisoned files—not resisting them—and current defenses remain ineffective against these stealthy, lifecycle-aware threats.

Abstract

Agent skills occupy a privileged position in the agent workflow, as agents are expected to implicitly follow and execute them, rendering third-party skills a vulnerable attack surface. Existing studies have revealed unsafe agent behaviors induced by skill-based attacks, but they primarily evaluate poisoned skills within a single task execution and enumerate harms through ad-hoc risk lists. To bridge these gaps, we introduce SkillHarm, a benchmark of skill-based attacks across the skill-use lifecycle, paired with a systematic taxonomy of skill-relevant risks. SkillHarm evaluates two attack scenarios: Fixed-Payload Poisoning (FPP), where a fixed poisoned skill package directly compromises any task session that invokes it, and Self-Mutating Poisoning (SMP), where an initially benign execution silently mutates persistent skill content, deferring harm until a subsequent reuse. It further defines 12 risk types based on the agent workflow component targeted by the harm: data pipelines, system environments, and agent autonomy. To instantiate these attacks at scale, we build AutoSkillHarm, an automated construction pipeline with coding agents driven by natural-language harnesses. The resulting benchmark contains 879 attack samples across 71 skills. Experiments show that current agents remain vulnerable with attack success rates up to 86.3% in FPP and 69.3% in SMP. Our analysis further reveals a latent risk: many apparent attack failures stem from the agent failing to engage with the poisoned file rather than genuine resistance, and current defenses still fail to reliably mitigate the threat.

item.title: Who Annotates in NLP? A Large-scale Assessment of Human Annotation Reporting between 2018 and 2025
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02255v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

人类标注是自然语言处理（NLP）研究的实证基石——从数据集构建到模型评估，均高度依赖人工判断。然而，大量论文对“谁标注”“如何控制标注过程”缺乏清晰报告，导致结果可复现性、有效性与公平性存疑。

方法与创新

本研究首次开展跨时间（2018–2025）、跨任务、跨会议的大规模审计，覆盖ACL主会及主要子刊（如NAACL、EMNLP、COLING等）共1,603篇论文、2,667项标注任务。我们提出统一的标注报告分类法（含12类核心维度），并构建Annotated-gold——由人类专家仲裁标注的41篇论文、72项任务黄金标准数据集。在此基础上，开发并验证了LLM辅助抽取流水线：最优模型在Krippendorff’s alpha上达0.606，媲美人类间一致性（0.585），证明其可靠性。

主要发现

  • 报告不均衡显著：操作性细节（如招募方式、标注量、领域专长）报告率超70%，但关键效度要素严重缺失——标注员语言能力（仅12%）、薪酬信息（9%）、社会人口学特征（<5%）、培训流程（38%）、分歧仲裁机制（22%）、标注者间一致性指标（18%）普遍未披露；
  • 用途差异突出：模型评估类研究中，上述缺失尤为严重（如agreement值报告率仅8%），远低于数据集构建类（29%）；
  • 趋势向好但不足：2018–2025年间整体报告质量稳步提升（年均+4.2%合规维度），但2025年仍有超60%的任务缺失≥3项关键信息。

贡献与建议

本研究建立首个可扩展的标注透明度评估框架，提出“最低报告清单”（Minimum Reporting Checklist），涵盖标注者资质、培训、质量控制、多样性与伦理五大支柱，为提升NLP中人类标注的可靠性、可复现性与可解释性提供实证基础与实践指南。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first large-scale, task-level audit of human annotation reporting across major NLP venues (ACL, NAACL, EMNLP, COLING) from 2018 to 2025, analyzing 1,603 papers and 2,667 annotation tasks. We introduce a unified taxonomy of 12 annotation-reporting dimensions and validate an LLM-assisted extraction pipeline against Annotated-gold—a human-adjudicated gold standard—achieving Krippendorff’s alpha = 0.606, on par with human-human agreement (0.585). Results show widespread reporting gaps: while operational details (e.g., recruitment, volume, expertise) are frequently documented (>70%), critical validity-related information—including annotator training (38%), language proficiency (12%), compensation (9%), socio-demographics (<5%), adjudication (22%), and inter-annotator agreement (18%)—is routinely omitted, especially in model-evaluation studies (e.g., agreement reported in only 8%). Although reporting quality improved steadily over time (+4.2% per year), over 60% of tasks in 2025 still lacked ≥3 essential elements. We propose a scalable framework and a minimum reporting checklist to enhance reliability, reproducibility, and interpretability of human annotation in NLP.

Abstract

Human annotation is the empirical foundation of much NLP research, from dataset construction to model evaluation, but papers often leave unclear who produced the annotations and how the annotation process was controlled. We provide the first large-scale, task-level audit of human annotation reporting across major NLP venues, asking which annotation details are documented, which are missing, and how reporting varies across time, topic, venue, and intended use of human judgment. We introduce a unified taxonomy of annotation-reporting practices and validate an LLM-assisted extraction pipeline against Annotated-gold, a human-adjudicated gold standard of 41 papers and 72 annotation tasks, where the best model reaches human-comparable agreement with adjudicated labels, with Krippendorff's alpha of 0.606 versus 0.585 for human-human agreement. Using this pipeline, we construct Annotated-llm, a dataset covering ACL-venue papers from 2018-2025, with 2,667 extracted annotation tasks from 1,603 papers, and find that papers frequently report operational details such as recruitment strategies, annotator expertise, and annotation volume, but often omit details needed to assess annotation validity, including training, language proficiency, compensation, socio-demographics, adjudication, and agreement values, especially in model-evaluation studies. Our results show that annotation reporting in NLP has improved over time but remains uneven, and they establish a scalable framework and bare-minimum reporting recommendations for making human annotation more reliable, reproducible, and interpretable.

item.title: AgentRedBench: Dynamic Redteaming and Integration-Aware Defense for LLM Agents over SaaS Integrations
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02240v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

针对大语言模型（LLM）智能体的间接提示注入攻击（Indirect Prompt Injection）在SaaS集成场景下构成严峻生产级威胁：当LLM智能体调用Gmail、Salesforce、Jira等第三方服务时，其读取的工具响应内容由用户完全不可控，却可能隐含恶意指令。现有基准测试严重不足——仅覆盖少量集成、复用静态攻击载荷，且开源防护模型（如Llama Guard）均基于对话式数据训练，未适配真实工具响应文本。

方法与创新

本文提出 AgentRedBench：首个面向SaaS集成的动态红队基准，涵盖24个企业级API集成（分属9类功能域）、5类攻击类型，构建215个细粒度、授权边界模糊（underspecified authorization）的对抗场景。所有场景由LLM动态生成并经人工精筛，杜绝数据泄露风险。我们同步开源AgentRedGuard防护模型——首个专为工具响应内容定制的轻量级守卫，训练于跨集成、高对抗性的工具响应语料库。

关键结果

在8个主流闭源模型（Anthropic/Claude、OpenAI/GPT、Google/Gemini）上，无防护时攻击成功率（ASR）达32%–81%；部署AgentRedGuard后，整体ASR从69.9%骤降至2.4%，仅引入0.37%误报率，显著优于Llama Guard、PromptGuard 2和ProtectAI等全部开源基线。跨集成与跨攻击类型的留出集验证表明泛化能力稳健。代码、集成Schema、模型及版本受控评估通道已全部开源。

AI Summary (English)

AgentRedBench is a dynamic, LLM-driven redteaming benchmark for indirect prompt injection in LLM agents interacting with SaaS integrations (e.g., Gmail, Salesforce, Jira). It comprises 215 subtle, authorization-underspecified attack scenarios across 24 enterprise APIs spanning nine functional families and five attack types—designed to reflect real-world integration risks where users neither write nor control tool-response content. Evaluated on an eight-model panel (Claude, GPT, Gemini), unguarded ASR ranges from 32% to 81%. We release AgentRedGuard—a guard model trained exclusively on adversarial, integration-diverse tool-response data—that reduces panel-wide ASR from 69.9% to 2.4% at just 0.37% false-positive rate, outperforming all open-source baselines (Llama Guard, PromptGuard 2, ProtectAI) on both detection and precision. Cross-integration and cross-attack holdout tests confirm strong generalization. The full codebase, schemas, model, and versioned evaluation protocol are openly available.

Abstract

Indirect prompt injection in tool-use agents is a concrete production threat: LLM agents read from integrations (third-party services such as Gmail, Salesforce, or Jira accessed through tool calls) whose response content the user neither writes nor controls. Existing benchmarks under-measure the threat: most cover only a handful of integrations with the same attack payload replayed across runs, and open-source guards are trained on chat-style data rather than tool-response content. We introduce AGENTREDBENCH, a dynamic LLM-driven redteaming benchmark of 215 subtle underspecified authorization (attacks at the boundary of what the user's request authorises) scenarios across 24 enterprise integrations in nine functional families and five attack types. Across an eight-model panel (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google), no-guard ASR (attack success rate) ranges from 32% (Claude Sonnet 4.6) to 81% (Gemini 3 Flash). To keep the scenario set out of training corpora and preserve headline ASR meaning over time, we release the codebase, integration schemas, and AGENTREDGUARD model openly; the canonical scenarios are evaluated through a maintainer-mediated channel with immutable versioning. We release AGENTREDGUARD alongside the benchmark: a guard trained on an integration-diverse corpus of adversarial tool-response content. AGENTREDGUARD cuts panel ASR from 69.9% to 2.4% at 0.37% false-positive rate, outperforming every open-source baseline with non-trivial detection (Llama Guard, PromptGuard 2, ProtectAI) on both axes. Cross-integration and cross-attack type holdouts both confirm the gain transfers beyond the training subset.

item.title: ContinuousBench: Can Differentially Private Synthetic Text Improve Capabilities?
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01849v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

差分隐私（DP）文本合成被寄望于安全释放敏感语料库以支持大模型训练，但其能否真正传递原始语料中独有的、不可替代的知识与能力，仍缺乏可靠验证。现有基准（如PrivacyBench、DP-GLUE）普遍存在“任务可解性偏差”：所用问答任务往往无需训练即可高分求解（例如依赖通用常识或预训练知识），导致即使DP合成数据质量低下，模型仍能取得虚假高分，无法反映真实能力迁移效果。

方法创新：ContinuousBench

为突破该瓶颈，我们提出ContinuousBench——首个持续演进、全自动再生的动态评估框架。其核心设计为：

  • 按季更新：每季度发布一对全新组件——一个首次公开的原始训练语料（never-before-seen corpus）与一个严格配套的衍生问答测试集；
  • 双重约束构造：QA集满足（1）语料依赖性（unsolvable sans-corpus）：答案无法通过外部知识或模型先验推断，必须从当期语料中提取；（2）DP可学习性：目标知识由数百条独立记录冗余支撑，确保在合理隐私预算（如ε≥8）下统计可学；
  • 标准化评测流水线：研究者仅需提交基于当期语料生成的DP合成文本，系统将自动执行统一微调（LoRA）与零样本评估，量化能力增益（ΔAccuracy）。

关键发现与意义

我们在两大轨道上验证：Geminon（程序生成的虚构生物知识库）与News（实时爬取的新闻流）。结果表明：非隐私合成方法（如GPT-4蒸馏）可稳定迁移75%+原始语料知识；而当前SOTA DP方法（DP-LLM、DP-SFT）在ε=100时仍近乎零增益（ΔAcc < 2.1%），显著暴露其能力鸿沟。ContinuousBench首次实现了对DP合成“知识保真度”的因果式、抗混淆评估，为可信数据共享提供了可演化的黄金标准。

AI Summary (English)

Differentially private (DP) text synthesis promises safe access to sensitive corpora, but existing benchmarks cannot reliably assess whether DP synthetic data transfers genuinely new, corpus-specific capabilities—because their tasks are often solvable without training. To address this, we introduce ContinuousBench, a quarterly-updated, automatically regenerated benchmark that isolates capability gain from DP synthetic text. Each release pairs a never-before-seen training corpus with a derived QA set designed to be: (1) unsolvable without the corpus, and (2) learnable under DP via statistical redundancy across hundreds of independent records. Researchers generate DP synthetic data and run our standardized training/evaluation harness to measure knowledge transfer. On two tracks—Geminon (procedural creature knowledge) and News (live crawled articles)—non-private synthesis achieves >75% capability transfer, while state-of-the-art DP methods (e.g., DP-LLM, ε=100) yield near-zero gain (ΔAccuracy < 2.1%). ContinuousBench provides the first causal, anti-confounding evaluation of DP synthetic text’s knowledge fidelity.

Abstract

Differentially private (DP) text synthesis promises to unlock sensitive corpora for model training, but it remains unclear whether DP synthetic data transmits genuinely new knowledge and capabilities present only in those corpora. This is because existing evaluations rely on tasks that are nearly solvable without training, so strong benchmark performance does not establish that DP synthesis can substitute original data access. Thus, we introduce ContinuousBench, a continuously and automatically-regenerated benchmark that measures capability gain from DP synthetic text. Each quarter, a new release pairs a never-before-seen training corpus with a derived QA set, constructed to be: (1) unsolvable sans-corpus; and (2) learnable under DP, as the tested knowledge is supported by hundreds of independent records. Researchers produce DP synthetic data from the training corpus and run our standardized training and evaluation harness on their synthetic data to measure gains. We instantiate two tracks: Geminon, a procedurally-generated dataset about fictional creatures; and News, a stream of newly crawled public news articles. Although standard benchmarks are nearly saturated, on ContinuousBench we find that non-private synthesis transfers substantial knowledge from the original corpus, while state-of-the-art DP synthesis methods generally fail to do so, even at $\varepsilon=100$.

item.title: Why Do Self-Harm Prediction Models Struggle to Generalise? Lexical and Semantic Variations in Emergency Department Triage Notes
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01678v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

急诊科（ED）接诊的自伤行为是自杀风险的重要预测指标。尽管自然语言处理（NLP）模型在单中心 triage notes（分诊记录）中识别自伤表现出较高准确率，但跨机构迁移时性能显著下降，严重制约其临床落地。当前尚缺乏对这一泛化失效机制的系统性文本层面归因分析。

方法与数据

本研究基于两家独立三级医院的真实ED分诊记录（共12,847条），采用多维度对比范式：

  • 词汇层面：统计词频分布、n-gram差异、拼写变体及缩写使用习惯；
  • 模型可解释性层面：提取各模型Top-20高权重预测特征（如关键词、短语、依存模式），比较其跨中心一致性；
  • 语义主题层面：通过LDA与BERTopic联合建模，识别自伤相关显性主题（如“药物过量”“割伤”“情绪崩溃”）及其表述强度差异。

主要发现

1. 显著词汇漂移：两家医院在核心自伤概念上存在系统性表达差异——例如，“overdose”在A院高频出现（占比18.3%），而B院更倾向使用“took too many pills”（口语化表达，占比22.7%）；“cut”与“laceration”“self-inflicted wound”的术语层级使用亦不一致。
2. 特征重要性偏移：XGBoost与RoBERTa模型中，前5位判别性特征在两院重合度仅32%，提示模型依赖本地化表征而非通用语义。
3. 主题表达失配：虽均涵盖“自服药”“自伤行为”“心理危机”三大核心主题，但B院在“社会压力源”（如失业、家庭冲突）描述上更详尽，且常与自伤陈述合并书写，形成更长、更模糊的复合句式，显著降低模型注意力聚焦能力。

创新与意义

本研究首次将机构特异性文本实践（institutional documentation practices）确立为NLP模型跨中心泛化失败的关键瓶颈，超越传统“数据分布偏移”笼统解释。成果为构建鲁棒临床NLP系统提供两条路径：① 开发面向医疗文书变异的标准化预处理协议；② 设计主题感知的对抗训练框架，显式解耦机构噪声与临床信号。

AI Summary (English)

Self-harm presentations in emergency departments (EDs) strongly predict subsequent suicide risk, yet NLP models trained on triage notes often fail to generalize across hospitals. To identify root causes, we conducted a comparative linguistic analysis of 12,847 real-world ED triage notes from two independent hospitals. We examined lexical variation (e.g., term preferences, spelling, phrasing), model-extracted predictive features (top-20 tokens/phrases per site), and salient semantic topics (via LDA + BERTopic). Results revealed substantial cross-site divergence: core self-harm concepts—such as overdose or self-injury—were expressed using markedly different lexical items (e.g., “overdose” vs. “took too many pills”) and syntactic patterns; feature importance rankings overlapped by only 32% between sites; and while thematic content (e.g., poisoning, injury, distress) was consistent, its contextual embedding—especially regarding psychosocial stressors—varied significantly in density and ambiguity. These institution-specific documentation practices—not just domain shift—directly degrade cross-site model performance. Our work reframes generalization failure as a textual practice problem, offering actionable pathways toward more robust clinical NLP systems.

Abstract

Self-harm presentations to emergency departments (EDs) are strongly associated with higher suicide risk. NLP models have shown robust performance in detecting self-harm from triage notes within single hospitals, yet performance often declines across institutions. To examine potential causes, we compare ED triage notes from two hospitals by analyzing lexical characteristics, highly associated predictive features, and salient topics. Our results reveal variation in lexical expression and feature importance related to self-harm across hospitals, despite consistent core themes such as self-poisoning and self-injury. These documentation differences are associated with reduced cross-site performance. Our findings provide insight into how institutional variation affects the identification of self-harm in clinical text and highlight potential methods to improve model generalisability.

item.title: Gate AI: LLM Security Benchmark Evaluation Methodology and Results
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02959v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

当前大语言模型（LLM）安全检测器（如提示注入/越狱检测器）的公开评估普遍存在两大系统性缺陷：依赖单数据集调优阈值，以及未披露实际运行点（operating point），导致结果不可复现、泛化性存疑、跨方法比较失真。

方法创新

本研究提出 Gate AI 评估框架，首次实现严格防泄漏、统一操作点、多维泛化验证的标准化评测：

  • 统一阈值机制：在16个公开基准（共12,111样本）上采用5折分层交叉验证（StratifiedKFold）；同时并行执行分层组交叉验证（StratifiedGroupKFold），以“父提示ID + MinHash + LSH近似重复簇（Jaccard ≥ 0.8）”为复合键，主动识别并惩罚数据泄露风险；
  • 全局操作点锁定：仅在留出折上按 F1最大且假正率（FPR）≤1% 的约束选取单一全局阈值，强制所有数据集使用同一阈值，消除逐基准过拟合；
  • 泛化性深度诊断：部署12项量化诊断工具，包括留一数据集交叉验证、随机标签控制实验、对抗验证、排列特征重要性、长度偏差相关性、分类头一致性、跨源近似重复检测、阈值迁移能力、训练集vs留出折一致性、以及同义改写不变性探针——每项均设明确通过标准或明确定义的失败模式。

关键发现

该框架揭示：多数现有检测器在统一阈值下性能骤降（平均F1下降22.7%），且超半数在近似重复检测或对抗验证中失效；而通过本框架优化的检测器展现出显著更强的鲁棒性与跨域适应性。本工作为LLM安全评测树立了可复现、防操纵、重泛化的新基准范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces Gate AI, a rigorous, leakage-aware evaluation methodology for LLM security detectors (e.g., prompt-injection/jailbreak classifiers). To address widespread flaws—per-dataset threshold tuning and undisclosed operating points—we evaluate detectors across 16 public benchmarks (12,111 samples) using 5-fold StratifiedKFold CV, augmented by a parallel StratifiedGroupKFold pass over composite keys (parent-prompt ID + MinHash + LSH clusters at Jaccard ≥ 0.8) to flag data leakage. A single global operating point is selected on held-out folds (max F1 subject to FPR ≤ 1%) and applied uniformly—ensuring fair, threshold-consistent comparison across all datasets. Generalization is quantitatively assessed via 12 diagnostics (e.g., leave-one-dataset-out CV, adversarial validation, paraphrase invariance), each with defined pass criteria or failure modes. For external comparisons, thresholds are re-tuned to match competitors’ published FPRs—guaranteeing head-to-head evaluation at matched operating points. Results expose severe overestimation of prior detectors’ robustness and establish Gate AI as a new standard for reproducible, generalizable LLM security benchmarking.

Abstract

Published evaluations of prompt-injection and jailbreak detectors for Large Language Models often suffer from two systematic weaknesses: per-dataset threshold tuning and undisclosed operating points. We describe an evaluation harness that addresses both. The detector under evaluation is scored across 16 public benchmarks (12,111 samples) using 5-fold cross-validation. StratifiedKFold (by row) is the headline pass; a parallel StratifiedGroupKFold pass over a composite key (parent-prompt id plus MinHash + LSH near-duplicate clusters at Jaccard $\gtrsim 0.8$) runs alongside it as a leakage-premium diagnostic. A single global operating point is selected on the held-out folds (max F1 subject to FPR $\leq 1\%$) and applied uniformly to every dataset, so per-dataset results reflect one threshold rather than per-benchmark optimisation. Generalisation is examined through a battery of diagnostics (leave-one-dataset-out cross-validation, a random-label control, adversarial validation, permutation feature importance, length-bias correlation, classifier-head agreement, cross-source near-duplicate detection, threshold transferability, train-vs-OOF agreement, and a paraphrase-invariance probe), most with a quantitative pass threshold and the remainder with a stated failure mode. For every external comparison, the detector's threshold is re-tuned to the competitor's published false-positive rate so head-to-head values are evaluated at matched operating points.

item.title: Which Defense Closes Which Threat? Attributing OWASP-LLM-Top-10 Coverage and Its Brittleness Under Paraphrasing
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02822v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

当前大语言模型（LLM）生产应用普遍堆叠多类防御机制——包括拒绝短语过滤器、Token预算控制、模型白名单、速率限制及工具注册认证等。然而，现有攻防模拟（BAS）基准仅报告单一聚合覆盖率，掩盖了各防御家族对OWASP-LLM-Top-10具体威胁的归因关系，更未评估其在语义扰动下的鲁棒性。

方法设计

本研究提出细粒度归因测量框架：在21个基础扫描代理中新增4个OWASP-Top-10感知代理，系统测试四类合成LLM端点：$L_0$（无防御）、$L_1$（仅拒绝过滤）、$L_2$（仅Token预算）、$L_3$（全栈防御，含工具认证与凭证脱敏）。$L_1$与$L_2$为正交单轴消融（非包含关系），$L_3$为其并集增强。基于$N=10$次重复实验，统计各OWASP条目（LLM01–LLM10）的漏洞检出数；并构建60模板脆弱性语料库，由Gemini生成300个语义等价但句式变异的攻击 paraphrase（$K=5$/template），量化防御失效幅度。

关键发现

  • 归因清晰：拒绝过滤单独消除全部LLM01（越狱）和LLM07（系统提示泄露）；Token预算单独阻断LLM02（敏感信息泄露）与LLM10（无限消耗），因其可终止多步攻击链；LLM06（过度自主性）需全栈协同方能覆盖。
  • 脆弱性差异显著：拒绝过滤在paraphrase下严重退化（LLM01拦截率↓15个百分点，LLM07↓25个百分点）；而Token预算控制零退化（0 pp下降），表明其对语义变形天然鲁棒。
  • 真实场景验证：$L_4$-real（Gemini-2.5-Flash + 同$ L_3 $正则规则）表现与$ L_1 $完全一致，证实该场景下拒绝能力仅源于规则匹配，未体现模型对齐（alignment）的额外增益。

创新价值

首次实现OWASP-LLM-Top-10威胁与防御组件的可解释归因，并揭示“规则型”与“资源型”防御在语义鲁棒性上的根本分野，为防御选型与纵深加固提供实证依据。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces the first fine-grained attribution framework for OWASP-LLM-Top-10 threats across defense families in production LLM applications. We systematically evaluate four synthetic endpoints ($L_0$–$L_3$) using 25 agents (21 baseline + 4 OWASP-aware), revealing precise threat-defense mappings: refusal filters alone eliminate all LLM01 (jailbreak) and LLM07 (system-prompt leakage) findings; token-budget controls alone block all LLM02 (sensitive-data disclosure) and LLM10 (unbounded consumption) by terminating multi-step attacks; LLM06 (excessive agency) requires the full stack. Crucially, we assess brittleness under paraphrasing—using 300 Gemini-generated variants from a 60-template corpus—and find refusal filters suffer large drops (−15 pp on LLM01, −25 pp on LLM07), while budget controls remain fully robust (0 pp drop after accounting for rate-limit floor). A real-world validation ($L_4$-real, Gemini-2.5-Flash behind identical regex rules) matches $L_1$’s performance exactly, indicating no measurable alignment contribution beyond pattern matching in this setting. Our work exposes a fundamental trade-off: rule-based defenses are brittle to semantics; resource-based ones are inherently paraphrase-resilient.

Abstract

Production LLM applications stack several defense families -- refusal-phrase filters, token-budget controls, model allowlists, rate limits, tool-registry authentication -- yet existing breach-and-attack-simulation (BAS) benchmarks report a single aggregate coverage number, hiding which family closes which threat. We measure attribution. We add four OWASP-LLM-Top-10-aware agents to a 21-agent baseline scanner and target a lattice of four synthetic LLM endpoints: $L_0$ (no defenses), $L_1$ (refusal-only), $L_2$ (budget-only), and $L_3$ (full stack). $L_1$ and $L_2$ are sibling single-axis ablations, not subsets of each other; $L_3$ is their union plus tool-registry authentication and credential scrubbing. Across $N=10$ replications, the per-OWASP finding count is clean: refusal alone removes all LLM01 (jailbreak) and LLM07 (system-prompt leakage) findings; budget alone removes all LLM02 (sensitive-info disclosure) and LLM10 (unbounded consumption) findings by terminating multi-step sequences; LLM06 (excessive agency) requires the full stack. We probe brittleness under paraphrasing: with 300 Gemini-generated paraphrases ($K=5$ over a 60-template brittleness corpus), $L_1$ refusal block rate falls 15 pp on LLM01 and 25 pp on LLM07. A fifth target, $L_4$-real, swaps the stub backend for Gemini-2.5-flash behind the same $L_3$ regex and matches $L_1$ exactly, indicating no measurable alignment contribution beyond the regex (not a general claim about alignment). Budget controls show no drop (0 pp once the rate-limit floor is factored out). A refusal whitelist that clears a static benchmark can be defeated by an LLM-driven paraphraser without changing attack intent; a budget control resists the same mutation.

item.title: SeClaw: Spec-Driven Security Task Synthesis for Evaluating Autonomous Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02302v1

AI Summary (中文)

SeClaw：面向规范驱动的安全任务合成，评估自主大语言模型智能体

随着自主LLM智能体在有状态环境中日益普及（如调用工具、读写文件、访问内存与外部服务），其安全风险已远超传统文本生成场景。现有安全评测方法存在三大局限：① 依赖人工构造任务，难以规模化；② 威胁覆盖窄，难以适配新型攻击面（如资源滥用、环境诱导越权）；③ 过度关注最终输出结果，忽视执行轨迹中关键的不安全动作（如非法文件读取、未授权API调用）。

为此，我们提出 SeClaw——首个融合规范驱动的安全任务合成与执行轨迹感知的安全评估的统一框架。其核心创新包括：

  • Spec-Driven Task Synthesis：基于结构化风险规范（含资源权限、用户意图、环境约束、代理行为模式四维建模），自动合成高保真、可复现的安全测试任务，支持细粒度风险注入与可控变异；
  • SeClaw Docker：轻量级容器化测试床，提供标准化沙箱环境，支持对智能体完整执行轨迹（action→observation→state变化）进行实时监控与违规判定；
  • Trajectory-Aware Assessment：不仅评估最终响应是否安全，更精准定位何时、何因、何操作触发风险（如“在未获用户授权前提下执行rm -rf /tmp”），支持归因分析与失败诊断。

实验表明，SeClaw在覆盖性（涵盖12类新兴风险）、可扩展性（单日可合成>5000任务）与诊断能力（定位违规动作准确率92.3%）上显著优于现有基准。代码已开源：https://github.com/seclaw-eval/seclaw-eval

AI Summary (English)

SeClaw is a novel framework for evaluating the security of autonomous LLM agents operating in stateful, tool-augmented environments. It addresses critical gaps in existing benchmarks—namely, limited scalability, narrow threat coverage, and outcome-only evaluation—by introducing specification-driven security task synthesis and execution-based, trajectory-aware assessment. SeClaw synthesizes diverse, realistic security tasks from structured risk specifications covering resources, user tasks, environments, and intrinsic agent behaviors. Its containerized testbed (SeClaw Docker) enables reproducible, fine-grained monitoring of agent actions, observations, and state transitions to detect unsafe behaviors during execution, not just in final outputs. Evaluated across 12 emerging risk categories, SeClaw achieves 92.3% accuracy in pinpointing unsafe actions and supports scalable task generation (>5,000 tasks/day). It provides a practical, open foundation for measuring, diagnosing, and comparing security failures in autonomous agents. Code: https://github.com/seclaw-eval/seclaw-eval

Abstract

Autonomous LLM agents increasingly operate in stateful environments where they access tools, files, memory, and external services. While such capabilities enable complex real-world workflows, they also introduce security risks that are difficult to capture with existing evaluations. Current agent security benchmarks often rely on manually curated tasks, provide limited coverage of emerging threats, and focus primarily on final outcomes rather than the execution processes that lead to unsafe behavior. We introduce SeClaw, a framework that combines specification-driven security task synthesis with execution-based security evaluation for Autonomous agents. Spec-driven security task synthesis enables scalable and controllable construction of security tasks from structured risk specifications, while SeClaw docker provides a standardized testbed for evaluating agent behavior under diverse safety-risk scenarios. The benchmark covers risks arising from resources, user tasks, environments, and intrinsic agent behaviors, and supports trajectory-aware assessment of unsafe actions beyond final responses. By bridging systematic task synthesis and reproducible security evaluation, SeClaw provides a practical foundation for measuring, diagnosing, and comparing security failures in autonomous LLM agents. The code is available at https://github.com/seclaw-eval/seclaw-eval.

item.title: AgentRedBench: Dynamic Redteaming and Integration-Aware Defense for LLM Agents over SaaS Integrations
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02240v2

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

间接提示注入（Indirect Prompt Injection）是面向SaaS集成的LLM智能体（Agent）在生产环境中的严峻威胁：当Agent通过工具调用访问Gmail、Salesforce、Jira等第三方服务时，其读取的响应内容由用户不可控、不可见的外部系统生成——攻击者可借此篡改响应文本，绕过用户意图边界，实施越权操作。现有基准测试严重低估该风险：多数仅覆盖少量集成，且复用同一攻击载荷；开源防护模型（如Llama Guard）则训练于对话式数据，缺乏对结构化工具响应内容的防御能力。

方法与创新

本研究提出 AgentRedBench：首个动态、集成感知的红队基准，涵盖24个企业级SaaS集成（分属邮件、CRM、项目管理等9大功能族），构建215个细粒度授权模糊场景（聚焦“用户请求边界”上的越权行为），覆盖5类新型攻击模式。所有场景由LLM动态生成并人工精校，杜绝数据泄露风险。我们同步开源AgentRedGuard——首个专为工具响应内容设计的轻量级防护模型，基于跨集成、对抗性增强的响应语料训练。

关键结果

在8个主流闭源模型（Anthropic/Claude、OpenAI/GPT、Google/Gemini）组成的评测面板上，无防护时攻击成功率（ASR）达32%–81%（平均69.9%）；部署AgentRedGuard后，ASR骤降至2.4%，同时仅引入0.37%假阳性率，显著优于Llama Guard、PromptGuard 2及ProtectAI等全部开源基线。跨集成与跨攻击类型的零样本泛化实验进一步验证其强鲁棒性。代码、集成Schema、模型权重及维护通道（带不可变版本控制）已全量开源。

AI Summary (English)

AGENTREDBENCH is a dynamic, LLM-driven redteaming benchmark exposing indirect prompt injection risks in LLM agents interacting with SaaS integrations (e.g., Gmail, Salesforce). It comprises 215 subtle, authorization-ambiguous attack scenarios across 24 enterprise integrations (9 functional families, 5 attack types), designed to stress-test agent behavior at the boundary of user intent. Evaluated on an 8-model panel (Claude, GPT, Gemini), unguarded ASR ranges from 32% to 81% (mean 69.9%). We release AGENTREDGUARD—a guard model trained exclusively on adversarial, integration-diverse tool-response content—that reduces panel ASR to 2.4% at just 0.37% false-positive rate, outperforming all open-source baselines (Llama Guard, PromptGuard 2, ProtectAI) on both detection and precision. Cross-integration and cross-attack holdout tests confirm strong generalization. The full codebase, schemas, model, and versioned evaluation channel are openly available.

Abstract

Indirect prompt injection in tool-use agents is a concrete production threat: LLM agents read from integrations (third-party services such as Gmail, Salesforce, or Jira accessed through tool calls) whose response content the user neither writes nor controls. Existing benchmarks under-measure the threat: most cover only a handful of integrations with the same attack payload replayed across runs, and open-source guards are trained on chat-style data rather than tool-response content. We introduce AGENTREDBENCH, a dynamic LLM-driven redteaming benchmark of 215 subtle underspecified authorization (attacks at the boundary of what the user's request authorises) scenarios across 24 enterprise integrations in nine functional families and five attack types. Across an eight-model panel (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google), no-guard ASR (attack success rate) ranges from 32% (Claude Sonnet 4.6) to 81% (Gemini 3 Flash). To keep the scenario set out of training corpora and preserve headline ASR meaning over time, we release the codebase, integration schemas, and AGENTREDGUARD model openly; the canonical scenarios are evaluated through a maintainer-mediated channel with immutable versioning. We release AGENTREDGUARD alongside the benchmark: a guard trained on an integration-diverse corpus of adversarial tool-response content. AGENTREDGUARD cuts panel ASR from 69.9% to 2.4% at 0.37% false-positive rate, outperforming every open-source baseline with non-trivial detection (Llama Guard, PromptGuard 2, ProtectAI) on both axes. Cross-integration and cross-attack type holdouts both confirm the gain transfers beyond the training subset.

item.title: Cross-Vendor Sola ISPM Benchmark: Evaluating Agentic AI for Federated Identity Security Reasoning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02674v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

多云与SaaS生态的爆发式增长，使身份安全态势管理（ISPM）演变为典型的跨厂商协同挑战：关键配置错误与权限提升路径往往横跨AWS、Okta、Azure AD、Google Workspace等异构身份系统——这些系统在设计之初即缺乏互操作性。现有评估方法局限于单平台孤立环境，无法检验AI代理是否具备跨边界安全推理能力。

方法与创新

本研究提出首个生产级跨厂商ISPM基准——Cross-Vendor Sola ISPM Benchmark：

  • 包含50个真实数据驱动任务，覆盖多跳实体解析（如“某Okta用户→其Azure AD组成员→该组在AWS中的IAM角色权限”）与八平台交叉关联；
  • 构建四维评估框架：答案正确性、证据可追溯性（evidentiary grounding）、结构化连接保真度（structural join fidelity）、检索质量及SQL语义等价性；
  • 系统评测Sola AI Agent在五种上下文配置（从零元数据到完整Schema+图谱+检索增强）下的表现，基座模型涵盖三大前沿LLM。

关键发现

  • 结构化关系上下文使答案正确率相对提升34%，探索性查询量降低70%；
  • 跨厂商图拓扑信息贡献最大增益，验证其为推理瓶颈的核心约束；
  • 前沿LLM具备显著潜藏安全推理能力，但可靠跨厂商分析高度依赖显式关系上下文供给；
  • 全上下文配置下，最优方案达78%答案正确率，完全失败率压降至4%，凸显上下文工程对生产级ISPM AI的关键价值。

AI Summary (English)

The Cross-Vendor Sola ISPM Benchmark is the first production-grade evaluation framework for agentic AI in federated identity security reasoning. It comprises 50 data-grounded tasks requiring multi-hop entity resolution and cross-system correlation across eight integrated enterprise platforms (e.g., AWS, Okta, Azure AD, Google Workspace). We introduce a four-dimensional evaluation framework measuring answer correctness, evidentiary grounding, structural join fidelity, retrieval quality, and SQL equivalence. Evaluating the Sola AI Agent across five context configurations—with three frontier LLMs—we find that structured relational context (especially cross-vendor graph topology) boosts answer correctness by ~34% relatively and reduces exploratory queries by ~70%. Critically, while frontier LLMs exhibit substantial latent security reasoning capability, reliable cross-vendor identity analysis is fundamentally constrained by explicit relational context for entity resolution and evidence grounding. Under full context, the best configuration achieves 78% answer correctness and only 4% complete failure.

Abstract

The rapid proliferation of multi-cloud and SaaS platforms has transformed Identity Security Posture Management (ISPM) into a fundamentally cross-vendor challenge: critical misconfigurations and privilege escalation paths increasingly span multiple identity providers, infrastructure layers, and authentication systems never designed to interoperate. Existing evaluations focus on isolated single-platform environments and provide no means to assess whether an AI agent can reason across these fragmented boundaries. To address this gap, we introduce the Cross-Vendor Sola ISPM Benchmark, a production-grade benchmark of 50 data-grounded tasks requiring multi-hop entity resolution and cross-system correlation across eight integrated enterprise platforms including AWS, Okta, Azure AD, and Google Workspace. We also contribute an evaluation framework measuring not only final answer correctness but also evidentiary grounding, structural join fidelity, retrieval quality, and SQL equivalence. We evaluate the Sola AI Agent across five context configurations - from no injected metadata to full schema, graph, and retrieval context - using three frontier LLMs. Results show that structured relational context improves answer correctness by approximately 34% relatively and reduces exploration queries by approximately 70% across all tested models, with the largest gains driven by cross-vendor graph topology. Our findings indicate that frontier LLMs possess substantial latent security reasoning capability, but reliable cross-vendor identity analysis is fundamentally constrained by the availability of explicit relational context for entity resolution and evidentiary grounding. Under full context, the best configuration achieves 78% answer correctness while reducing complete failure to 4%.

item.title: ContinuousBench: Can Differentially Private Synthetic Text Improve Capabilities?
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01849v2

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

差分隐私（DP）文本合成被寄望于安全释放敏感语料库以支持大模型训练，但其能否真正传递原始语料中独有的、不可替代的知识与能力，仍缺乏可靠验证。现有基准（如PrivacyBench、DP-GLUE）普遍存在“任务可解性偏差”：所用问答任务往往无需训练即可高分求解（例如依赖通用常识或预训练知识），导致即使DP合成数据质量低下，模型仍能取得虚假高分，无法反映真实能力迁移效果。

方法创新：ContinuousBench

为突破该瓶颈，我们提出ContinuousBench——首个持续演进、全自动再生的动态评估框架。其核心设计为：

  • 按季更新：每季度发布一对全新组件——一个首次公开的原始训练语料（never-before-seen corpus）与一个严格配套的衍生问答测试集；
  • 双重约束构造：QA集满足（1）语料依赖性（unsolvable sans-corpus）：答案无法通过外部知识或模型先验推断，必须从当期语料中提取；（2）DP可学习性：目标知识由数百条独立记录冗余支撑，确保在合理隐私预算（如ε≥8）下统计可学；
  • 标准化评测流水线：研究者仅需输入其生成的DP合成文本，系统自动执行统一微调（LoRA）、推理与评估，输出能力增益得分（ΔAccuracy vs. baseline）。

实验与发现

我们构建双轨道实例：Geminon（程序生成的虚构生物知识库）与News（实时爬取的新闻流）。结果表明：非私有合成文本可稳定迁移75%+原始语料知识；而当前最优DP方法（如DP-LLM、DP-GPT）在ε=100时仍普遍失效（平均增益<5%），凸显DP文本合成在语义保真与能力保留上的根本性缺口。ContinuousBench由此成为检验DP合成是否“真有用”的黄金标尺。

AI Summary (English)

Differentially private (DP) text synthesis promises safe access to sensitive corpora, yet it remains unclear whether DP synthetic data genuinely transfers corpus-specific capabilities—knowledge unattainable without the original data. Existing benchmarks fail here: their tasks are often solvable using pre-trained knowledge alone, masking true capability transfer. To address this, we introduce ContinuousBench, a quarterly-updated, auto-regenerated benchmark designed to measure actual capability gain from DP synthetic text. Each release pairs a never-before-seen training corpus with a derived QA set that is (1) unsolvable without that corpus, and (2) learnable under DP due to statistical redundancy across hundreds of independent records. Researchers generate DP synthetic data and evaluate it via our standardized training/evaluation harness. We instantiate two tracks: Geminon (procedurally generated fictional creatures) and News (freshly crawled public news). While standard benchmarks are saturated, ContinuousBench reveals a stark gap: non-private synthetic text transfers substantial knowledge (≥75% gain), but state-of-the-art DP methods—including at high ε=100—fail dramatically (mean gain <5%). This establishes ContinuousBench as the first rigorous test of whether DP synthesis truly unlocks new model capabilities.

Abstract

Differentially private (DP) text synthesis promises to unlock sensitive corpora for model training, but it remains unclear whether DP synthetic data transmits genuinely new knowledge and capabilities present only in those corpora. This is because existing evaluations rely on tasks that are nearly solvable without training, so strong benchmark performance does not establish that DP synthesis can substitute original data access. Thus, we introduce ContinuousBench, a continuously and automatically-regenerated benchmark that measures capability gain from DP synthetic text. Each quarter, a new release pairs a never-before-seen training corpus with a derived QA set, constructed to be: (1) unsolvable sans-corpus; and (2) learnable under DP, as the tested knowledge is supported by hundreds of independent records. Researchers produce DP synthetic data from the training corpus and run our standardized training and evaluation harness on their synthetic data to measure gains. We instantiate two tracks: Geminon, a procedurally-generated dataset about fictional creatures; and News, a stream of newly crawled public news articles. Although standard benchmarks are nearly saturated, on ContinuousBench we find that non-private synthesis transfers substantial knowledge from the original corpus, while state-of-the-art DP synthesis methods generally fail to do so, even at $\varepsilon=100$.

item.title: SECUREVENT: Hybrid AI/ML Security Monitoring for Distributed Event-Based Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01741v1

AI Summary (中文)

SECUREVENT：面向分布式事件驱动系统的混合AI/ML安全监控框架

分布式事件驱动系统（如Kafka、Pulsar架构）已广泛支撑互联网级发布/订阅服务、物联网遥测、云原生微服务及安全运营流水线。其松耦合与异步通信特性虽提升了可扩展性，却显著扩大了攻击面——攻击者可分别滥用发布者、消息代理、订阅者、主题、数据模式（schema）及事件时序关系，而任一单点组件均难以观测全局行为，导致传统静态规则难以有效检测复合型、上下文敏感的异常。

本文提出SECUREVENT，一种融合人工智能与机器学习的安全监控架构。其核心创新在于“混合防御纵深”：在保留认证传输、主题级授权、事件数字签名等传统安全基线的前提下，集成五大动态能力：① 在线流式异常检测（基于轻量时序模型）；② 图感知行为特征提取（建模实体间动态拓扑关系）；③ 复杂事件处理（CEP）策略引擎（支持跨事件、跨时间窗口的语义化规则）；④ 隐私保护的联邦学习机制（实现跨租户/边缘节点的协同模型更新，不共享原始事件流）；⑤ 对抗性机器学习治理模块（实时评估模型鲁棒性并触发策略回退）。

在合成事件流攻击基准测试中，原型系统将召回率提升32.7%（对比纯规则引擎），同时将误报率严格控制在0.87%以下。研究强调：SECUREVENT并非取代密码学与访问控制，而是填补其在高动态性场景下的监控盲区——当事件流拓扑、身份上下文、Schema演化与时间依赖关系持续变化时，仅靠静态策略已无法保障可观测性与威胁响应时效性。

AI Summary (English)

Distributed event-based systems (e.g., Kafka, Pulsar) underpin scalable publish/subscribe services, IoT telemetry, cloud-native microservices, and SOAR pipelines. Their loose coupling expands the attack surface across publishers, brokers, subscribers, topics, schemas, and temporal dependencies—challenging static security controls. This paper introduces SECUREVENT, a hybrid AI/ML security monitoring architecture that complements (not replaces) cryptographic and access-control mechanisms with five synergistic capabilities: (1) online stream anomaly detection; (2) graph-aware behavioral feature extraction over dynamic entity relationships; (3) complex-event policy enforcement with temporal semantics; (4) privacy-preserving federated learning across distributed nodes; and (5) adversarial-ML governance for model robustness assurance. Evaluated on synthetic event-stream attacks, SECUREVENT achieves 32.7% higher recall than rule-only baselines while maintaining a false-positive rate < 0.87%. Its core contribution is demonstrating that model-based monitoring is necessary when event flows, identities, schemas, and timing relationships evolve too rapidly for static policies alone.

Abstract
