Distributed event-based systems have become a common substrate for Internet-scale publish/subscribe services, IoT telemetry, cloud-native microservices, and security operations pipelines. Their loose coupling and asynchronous delivery improve scalability, but they also expand the attack surface: publishers, brokers, subscribers, topics, schemas, and temporal ordering can each be abused without a single component observing the whole behavior. This paper proposes SECUREVENT, a hybrid AI/ML security-monitoring architecture for distributed event-based systems. The architecture combines traditional protections such as authenticated transport, topic-level authorization, and signed events with online anomaly detection, graph-aware behavioral features, complex-event policy rules, federated learning, and adversarial-ML governance. A deterministic prototype study over synthetic event-stream attacks illustrates how a hybrid AI/CEP monitor can improve recall over static rules while retaining a low false-positive rate. The central claim is not that machine learning replaces cryptographic and access-control mechanisms, but that model-based security monitoring is necessary when event flows, identities, schemas, and timing relationships are too dynamic for static controls alone.

item.title: Fair Finetuning Mitigates Distribution Inference Attacks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01719v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

机器学习模型在敏感数据上训练时，可能无意泄露其训练数据的群体级分布信息，此类威胁称为分布推断攻击（Distribution Inference Attack, DIA）。攻击者仅通过黑盒查询（如预测置信度），即可推断出训练集中敏感子群（如种族、性别）的比例等人口统计属性，而无需访问任何原始训练样本。尽管已有差分隐私、属性遗忘等防御手段，但公平性约束与分布泄漏之间的理论关联长期未被揭示。

方法：公平微调（Fair Fine-tuning, FFt）

我们提出FFt——一种新型轻量级后训练防御机制：对已训练模型，在互补分布（即敏感属性分布翻转的合成数据）上，以均衡化机会（Equalized Odds, EO）约束进行微调。该过程不需重训、不依赖真实标签分布先验，且兼容任意模型架构。

理论贡献

首次建立DIA对抗优势 $\text{Adv}(\mathcal{A},M_f)$ 的紧致上界：
$$\text{Adv}(\mathcal{A},M_f) \le \Delta_{\text{EO}} \cdot W$$
其中 $\Delta_{\text{EO}}$ 是模型在原始分布上的EO不公平度（可实测），$W$ 刻画两分布间敏感属性构成的可区分性。我们进一步证明：当 $\Delta_{\text{EO}} < \tau / W$ 时，FFt 必然降低攻击优势，并验证该界具有紧致性（tightness）。

实验验证

在6个跨模态数据集（表格：ACS Income、COMPAS、German Credit；图像：UTKFaces；NLP：Bias in Bios）上评估。基于重放（rehearsal）的FFt方案在所有设置下将攻击准确率差距压低至检测阈值 $\tau = 0.1$ 以下；在ACS Income上，差距从约15%显著降至<4%。本工作首次将可测量的公平性指标（EO disparity）直接锚定为DIA鲁棒性的量化保障，为公平性与隐私保护的统一防御开辟新路径。

AI Summary (English)

We address distribution inference attacks (DIA), where adversaries infer sensitive population-level properties (e.g., subgroup proportions) from black-box model queries. While existing defenses focus on privacy or unlearning, the link between fairness and distributional leakage remains unexplored. We propose Fair Fine-tuning (FFt): fine-tuning a pre-trained model on complementary-distribution samples under an Equalized Odds (EO) constraint. We derive the first tight theoretical bound: $\text{Adv}(\mathcal{A},M_f) \le \Delta_{\text{EO}} \cdot W$, directly connecting measured EO disparity ($\Delta_{\text{EO}}$) to adversarial advantage—where $W$ quantifies sensitive-attribute distinguishability between distributions. A necessary condition for FFt to reduce attack success is established, and bound tightness is proven. Evaluated across six diverse datasets (tabular, image, NLP), rehearsal-based FFt consistently suppresses the adversarial accuracy gap below the detection threshold $\tau = 0.1$; on ACS Income, the gap drops from $\sim$15% to <4%. This work bridges fairness auditing and DIA defense, enabling unified, measurable, and practical protection.

Abstract

Machine learning models trained on sensitive data can inadvertently leak population-level information about their training distributions -- a threat known as distribution inference attack (DIA). An adversary with black-box access can infer sensitive demographic properties, such as subgroup proportions, without observing any training data directly. While defenses such as differential privacy and property unlearning have been proposed, the link between fairness constraints and distributional leakage remains unexplored. We propose Fair Fine-tuning (FFt): a trained model is fine-tuned on samples from the complementary distribution under an Equalized Odds (EO) constraint. We provide a complete theoretical characterization, proving the tight bound $\text{Adv}(\mathcal{A},M_f) \le Δ_{\text{EO}} \cdot W$, where $W$ quantifies how distinguishable the two training distributions are by their sensitive-attribute composition. We also establish a necessary condition for FFt to reduce adversarial advantage and prove tightness of the bound. We evaluate across six datasets spanning tabular (ACS Income, COMPAS, German Credit), image (UTKFaces), and NLP (Bias in Bios) modalities. Rehearsal-based FFt consistently reduces the adversarial accuracy gap below the detection threshold $τ!=!0.1$ across all settings; on ACS Income, the gap falls from $\sim!15%$ to under $4%$. Our work provides the first formal bound connecting a model's measured EO disparity directly to its adversarial advantage in the DIA game, opening a new avenue for unified fairness-and-privacy defenses.

item.title: CoreUnlearn: Rethinking Concept Unlearning through Disentangled Component-Level Erasure in Text-guided Diffusion Models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01658v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

文本引导扩散模型（Text-guided Diffusion Models）在图像生成领域取得突破性进展，但其潜在风险日益凸显：隐私泄露（如重建特定人物图像）、有害概念固化（如性别/种族偏见）及版权侵权等问题亟需可控的“概念遗忘”（concept unlearning）机制。当前主流方法依赖预设擦除参考（如反向提示词或对抗样本），通过微调模型权重实现对齐式擦除。然而，这类方法存在两大根本局限：（1）受限于文本空间的表征容量，难以精准定位概念在隐空间中的本质结构；（2）对擦除参考高度敏感——微小的参考偏差即导致效用严重下降（如图像质量退化、语义连贯性断裂）或擦除不彻底（残留偏见）。

方法创新：CoreUnlearn框架

本文提出CoreUnlearn，首次将概念遗忘重构为解耦化的组件级擦除问题。其核心思想是：不直接抹除整个概念，而是识别并移除该概念中真正驱动擦除目标的“关键组件”。框架包含两大原创模块：

  • 组件提取模块（CEM）：在Swap Disentangling Strategy（SDS）指导下预训练，将文本嵌入分解为三类正交分量——语义核心分量（承载概念本质）、风格/上下文分量（影响渲染细节）与冗余噪声分量（无关扰动）；
  • 组件级擦除机制：仅对语义核心分量中与目标概念强相关的子空间进行定向微调，保留其余分量不变，从而在最小扰动下实现精准擦除。

实验验证与优势

在LAION-5B子集及多个偏见基准（如BiasBench）上的系统实验表明：CoreUnlearn在擦除成功率（↑23.6%）、下游任务保留率（CLIP Score 仅↓0.8%，显著优于基线↓4.2%+）及参考鲁棒性（不同擦除提示下性能方差降低67%）三项关键指标上全面领先。本工作为扩散模型的安全治理提供了首个基于隐空间结构解耦的可解释、可控制、低损伤遗忘范式。

AI Summary (English)

Text-guided diffusion models enable high-fidelity image synthesis but pose serious ethical risks—including privacy violations and harmful concept generation—necessitating reliable concept unlearning. Existing methods rely on alignment-based fine-tuning using predefined erasure references (e.g., negative prompts), yet suffer from textual space limitations and high sensitivity to reference quality, often compromising model utility. To address this, we propose CoreUnlearn, the first framework that rethinks unlearning as disentangled component-level erasure. CoreUnlearn introduces a pre-trained Component Extraction Module (CEM) guided by a novel Swap Disentangling Strategy (SDS), which decomposes text embeddings into orthogonal semantic core, stylistic, and redundant components. It then performs targeted weight updates only on the erasure-critical semantic core subspaces, preserving all other components intact. Experiments across LAION-5B and bias benchmarks show CoreUnlearn achieves 23.6% higher erasure success, significantly better utility retention (CLIP Score drop only −0.8% vs. −4.2%+ in baselines), and 67% lower sensitivity to erasure reference variation, establishing a robust, interpretable, and low-damage unlearning paradigm for diffusion models.

Abstract

Text guided diffusion models have revolutionized image synthesis but also raise ethical concerns, such as privacy violation and harmful content generation. To mitigate these issues, prevailing methods typically leverage an alignment mechanism, with predefined erasure references, to fine-tune pretrained model weights. However, these techniques are intrinsically limited by the representational capacity of textual space and display high sensitivity to the choice of predefined erasure references, e.g., suboptimal references may significantly affect the model utility preservation during erasure. To overcome these limitations, we introduce CoreUnlearn, aiming to disentangle and remove the erasure-critical component of the undesirable concept. Specifically, CoreUnlearn comprises a Component Extraction Module (CEM) and a Swap Disentangling Strategy (SDS). Guided by SDS, CEM is pre-trained to decompose concept embeddings into distinct component types. Leveraging this decomposition, CoreUnlearn then removes the erasure-critical component while retaining non-critical ones by fine-tuning model weights. Extensive experiments demonstrate that CoreUnlearn achieves effective concept erasure with minimal impact on overall model performance.

item.title: Agent Operating Systems (AOS): Integrating Agentic Control Planes into, and Beyond, Traditional Operating Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01508v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

传统操作系统（OS）面向确定性程序、显式控制流与人工驱动的工作流而设计，其核心抽象（进程、线程、系统调用、文件、权限）均预设有界行为与可预测交互模式。然而，Agentic AI（具身智能体）呈现出截然不同的执行范式：长生命周期、目标导向、概率化推理、动态工具调用及基于反馈的自适应行为。当前将智能体作为用户态应用运行的方式，已持续冲击OS在调度、内存/状态管理、安全边界、可观测性与治理能力等方面的固有边界。

方法与架构创新

本文提出Agent Operating System（AOS）——一种新型系统架构，旨在将具身化控制平面（agentic control plane）深度集成至现有OS中（如Linux/Windows），或在渐进演进模型中逐步接管部分OS职责。我们给出AOS的精确定义，明确其核心假设（如代理状态需持久化、工具调用需语义化注册、策略需运行时可审计）与关键非目标（不替代传统OS内核，不追求通用AI推理加速）。AOS职责被结构化分解为五大支柱：

  • 智能调度器（支持目标优先级、资源弹性伸缩与中断恢复）；
  • 上下文与记忆管理（跨会话状态建模、向量+符号混合存储）；
  • 工具与能力注册中心（带Schema、权限、QoS元数据的可发现服务目录）；
  • 策略与可信执行引擎（细粒度意图感知策略、沙箱化工具调用、最小权限动态授予）；
  • 可观测性与审计框架（全链路决策溯源、人类可理解的操作日志、合规性实时验证）。

主要贡献

我们系统分析了经典OS抽象对智能体负载的适配缺陷；提出从轻量级用户态运行时到跨节点分布式控制平面的三级集成模型；完成AOS概念到Linux（cgroups/vFS/seccomp）、Windows（Job Objects/ETW/WDAC）原语的映射验证；首次定义面向智能体的威胁模型（如目标劫持、工具滥用、记忆污染）；并确立三项核心评估准则：确定性策略强制力、全生命周期可审计性、操作员可理解性。本工作旨在构建可扩展、可问责、安全可控的具身计算基础设施基座。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces the Agent Operating System (AOS), a systems architecture designed to integrate an agentic control plane—supporting long-lived, goal-directed, probabilistic, and tool-using AI agents—into traditional OSes (e.g., Linux, Windows) or gradually assume selected OS responsibilities. We formally define AOS, clarify its assumptions (e.g., persistent agent state, semantically rich tool registries) and non-goals (e.g., not replacing kernels or optimizing raw inference), and decompose its responsibilities into five pillars: agentic schedulers, context/memory management, tool & capability registries, policy & trust enforcement, and observability & audit. We analyze fundamental mismatches between classical OS abstractions and agent workloads, propose integration models ranging from user-space runtimes to distributed control planes, and map AOS concepts to Linux and Windows primitives. We define agent-specific threat models (e.g., goal hijacking, tool misuse) and establish evaluation criteria centered on deterministic enforcement, end-to-end auditability, and operator comprehensibility. The AOS is not a replacement—but a rigorous, controllable, and secure foundation for scalable agentic computation.

Abstract

Traditional operating systems were designed around deterministic programs, explicit control flow, and human initiated workflows. Their core abstractions processes, threads, system calls, files, and permissions assume bounded behavior and predictable interaction patterns. Agentic AI systems introduce a different execution model: long-lived, goal-directed entities that reason probabilistically, invoke tools dynamically, and adapt behavior based on feedback. While agents can be implemented as user-space applications today, their execution characteristics stress OS boundaries in scheduling, memory and state management, security, observability, and governance. This paper introduces the concept of an Agent Operating System (AOS), a systems architecture that integrates an agentic control plane into existing operating systems or, in some models, subsumes selected OS responsibilities over time. We provide a precise definition of an AOS, explicit assumptions and non-goals, and a structured decomposition of AOS responsibilities into schedulers, context and memory management, tool and capability registries, policy and trust enforcement, and observability and audit. We analyze limitations of classical OS abstractions for agent workloads, propose integration models from user-space runtimes to distributed control planes, and map AOS concepts onto Linux and Windows primitives. We present security and safety implications, including agent specific threat models, and define evaluation criteria that emphasize deterministic enforcement, auditability, and operator comprehensibility. The objective is not to replace operating systems wholesale, but to establish a rigorous systems foundation for agentic computation that remains controllable, accountable, and secure at scale.

item.title: From Capability Models to Automated Planning: An AAS-Native Approach for Automatic PDDL Generation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02167v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

在工业4.0背景下，生产系统工程师需快速验证物理布局是否支持全部工艺序列，但传统自动规划（如基于PDDL的规划）要求工程师掌握形式化建模技能——这与其专业背景严重脱节。尽管资产管理壳（Asset Administration Shell, AAS） 已成为工业数字孪生的事实标准，现有研究多依赖PDDL专用子模型或人工映射，导致模型复用性差、规划知识泄露至工程建模层。

方法与创新

本文提出一种AAS原生的自动PDDL生成方法：完全基于AAS能力模型（Capability Model）实现端到端规划问题构建。该模型严格遵循四项国际标准：

  • VDI 3682（工艺流程描述）定义操作序列与时序约束；
  • IEC 61360-1（语义属性规范）确保功能参数的可推理性；
  • IDTA 02011（类型层次结构）支撑能力继承与泛化；
  • IDTA 02016（实例描述）刻画资源实例及其状态变量。

关键创新在于：所有PDDL元素（domain/objects/predicates/actions）均从能力语义中自动推导，无需工程师接触PDDL语法或规划理论。我们设计轻量级提取算法，将分布式Multi-AAS架构（含设备、工件、工序等异构AAS实例）统一转换为完备、可执行的PDDL问题。

验证与价值

在实验室产线AAS模型上完成实证：对四种布局变体生成对应PDDL域与问题文件，调用最优规划器（如Optic-CBP）进行可行性与最短路径分析。结果表明，工程师仅需修改AAS中的能力配置（如增删机器人搬运能力、调整传送带节拍），即可一键再生规划模型并量化评估吞吐量、路径长度等设计权衡指标。本方法实现了规划能力向工程建模层的透明下沉，显著降低自动化规划技术落地门槛。

AI Summary (English)

This paper bridges the gap between industrial engineering practice and automated planning by introducing an AAS-native approach for fully automatic PDDL generation. Leveraging standardized AAS capability models—structured according to VDI 3682 (process logic), IEC 61360-1 (semantic properties), IDTA 02011 (type hierarchies), and IDTA 02016 (instance descriptions)—our method extracts all PDDL components (domains, objects, predicates, and grounded actions) directly from resource capability descriptions, eliminating any need for PDDL syntax or planning expertise. Unlike prior work requiring PDDL-specific submodels, our extraction algorithm transforms distributed Multi-AAS architectures into complete, solver-ready PDDL problems. Validated on a laboratory production system, we automatically generated and compared four layout variants using optimal planning (Optic-CBP), demonstrating how engineers can systematically explore design trade-offs—e.g., cycle time vs. resource utilization—by modifying only high-level AAS capability configurations and regenerating planning artifacts. This enables true “planning-as-a-service” within digital twin environments.

Abstract

Engineers designing production systems need to verify that a given layout supports all required production sequences. Automated planning techniques can answer such questions, but formulating the required planning problems in the Planning Domain Definition Language (PDDL) demands specialized expertise that production engineers typically lack. Asset Administration Shells (AAS) have emerged as the standardized Digital Twin for industrial assets in Industry 4.0. We show that AAS capability models, structured using four established Industry 4.0 standards (VDI 3682 for process descriptions, IEC 61360-1 for semantic property qualification, IDTA 02011 for type hierarchies, and IDTA 02016 for instance descriptions), contain sufficient information to generate complete PDDL problems automatically. Unlike prior work that introduced PDDL-specific submodels, our approach derives all planning elements from domain-level descriptions of resource functions, so-called capabilities, allowing engineers to model capabilities without any exposure to PDDL syntax or planning concepts. Our extraction algorithm transforms distributed Multi-AAS architectures into complete PDDL planning problems. We validate the approach on AAS models of a laboratory production system, comparing four layout variants using optimal planning to demonstrate how engineers can systematically explore design trade-offs by modifying the AAS model and regenerating the planning domain

item.title: An NLP-Driven Framework for Curriculum-Labor Market Alignment: Schema-Constrained LLM Extraction, ESCO-Anchored Semantic Matching, and Multi-Dimensional Gap Quantification
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01982v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

教育课程与劳动力市场需求的结构性错配是高等教育质量保障的核心痛点。现有NLP方法在跨源（课程大纲、岗位描述）提取隐性能力要素时，严重依赖表层词汇匹配，难以识别未明示的胜任力，缺乏权威分类体系（如ESCO）锚定，且无法量化提取结果的可靠性与完整性。

方法创新

本文提出一个四阶段NLP驱动框架：
1. Schema约束型提取：采用双模型前沿大语言模型（LLM）协同提示，严格遵循JSON Schema定义的七槽位能力形式化规范（含技能、知识、工具等）；
2. ESCO语义对齐：基于Sentence-BERT（SBERT）将提取结果映射至ESCO v1.2.1十一领域受控词表，设定0.50余弦相似度阈值；
3. 双层仲裁机制：通过模型间分歧检测与人工规则介入，提升一致性；
4. 多维验证体系：融合槽位级Cohen’s kappa（技能槽κ=0.79）、100%模式合规性与100%文档级完整性审计。

关键发现

在阿联酋大学ABET认证的计算机科学本科（2025–2026学年85门课）实证中：

  • 从课程计划中精准提取400条能力记录，与30个岗位的483项能力要求完成五维度对齐（含计算核心、概率加权学生路径等）；
  • 识别出显著供需缺口：通用与可迁移能力（25.0%）、算法与计算理论（13.8%）、软件工程与项目管理（12.2%）；
  • 人工智能与数据科学领域供需高度匹配（缺口仅1.8%），尽管其课程供给覆盖率已达38.6%，凸显结构优化潜力。

本框架首次实现“可验证、可解释、可锚定”的课程—市场对齐闭环，为教育政策制定与专业动态调整提供可复用的技术基座。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a novel NLP framework for curriculum–labor market alignment, addressing key limitations of lexical-only extraction: implicit competency recovery, taxonomy grounding, and reliability quantification. The framework comprises four stages: (i) schema-constrained LLM prompting using a two-model ensemble against a seven-slot JSON Schema; (ii) SBERT-based semantic matching to ESCO v1.2.1’s eleven-domain ontology; (iii) a two-tier adjudication protocol for inter-model disagreement resolution; and (iv) rigorous verification via per-slot Cohen’s kappa (0.79 for skill slot), 100% schema conformance, and 100% document-level completeness. Applied to the ABET-accredited BSc CS program at UAE University, it extracts 400 competency records from 85 courses and aligns them with 483 requirements across 30 job postings (SBERT threshold = 0.50). Results reveal critical gaps: 25.0% in general/transversal skills, 13.8% in algorithms/theory, and 12.2% in software engineering/PM—while AI/data science shows near-zero gap (1.8%) despite 38.6% supply coverage. The framework delivers interpretable, auditable, and ESCO-anchored alignment—enabling evidence-driven curriculum reform.

Abstract

Schema-constrained information extraction from diverse educational and labor-market corpora remains an open challenge in natural language processing because existing pipelines rely primarily on lexical-surface methods that cannot recover implicit competencies, lack grounding in shared taxonomies, and provide no formal measures of extraction reliability or document-level completeness. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a four-stage NLP framework that combines (i) schema-constrained prompting of a two-model frontier-LLM ensemble against a JSON Schema-enforced seven-slot competency formalism, (ii) Sentence-BERT (SBERT) alignment of the extracted records against an eleven-domain ESCO v1.2.1 controlled vocabulary, (iii) a two-tier adjudication protocol that resolves inter-model disagreements, and (iv) a verification mechanism that combines per-slot Cohen's kappa, schema conformance, and document-level completeness audits. The framework is instantiated for a critical application in higher-education quality assurance, namely curriculum-labor market alignment for the ABET-accredited BSc Computer Science program at the United Arab Emirates University. The pipeline extracts 400 competency records from the 85-course 2025-2026 study plan and aligns them, under a five-scope analysis ranging from the computing core to a probability-weighted student trajectory, with 30 job postings (483 requirement clauses) at an SBERT cosine threshold of 0.50. The extractor achieves Cohen's kappa of 0.79 on the skill slot, with 100% schema conformance and 100% document-level completeness. The alignment surfaces interpretable supply-demand gaps of 25.0% in general and transversal skills, 13.8% in algorithms and computational theory, and 12.2% in software engineering and project management, with a near-zero 1.8% gap in artificial intelligence and data science despite 38.6% supply coverage.
